diceRolls.purpose: Seal check completion timing — does the exterior seal check finish before the docking hatch fully opens? Lower is better for LX-7. 1-6 = seal check completes in time, 7-10 = hatch opens first or simultaneously.
Which Ember Squad member enters first — 1=Rook, 2=Slate, 3=Verne, 4=Dasha Orin
Does Ember Squad immediately open fire, or do they hold? 1-3 = hold/assess, 4-6 = aggressive entry

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
worldFactsSnapshot.captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
worldFactsSnapshot.orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_maren_appearance: Maren Voss: mid-40s, dark circles, Helix Dynamics lanyard, clean clothes suggesting she arrived post-incident, holding a fire suppression wand as an improvised weapon
worldFactsSnapshot.module1_command_layout: Six stations, most dark. Captain's terminal centered against the far bulkhead — reinforced housing, still on backup power, status indicator pulsing red. Lower-ceilinged than Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
worldFactsSnapshot.captains_terminal_file_structure: Third partition contains two HX-7 classified Helix Dynamics files, timestamped fourteen months ago. Adjacent entries include operational logs and a file marked CREW MANIFEST — FINAL.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_hazard: Maren observed movement — low, slow — at the Module 3 end of the connector walkway between Module 2 and Module 3. Marks on the corridor walls at wrong height. She retreated and has not attempted the crossing again.
worldFactsSnapshot.module5_access_panel: Fused shut — confirmed by Maren's personal inspection after she boarded. She believes the specimen remains inside.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_carried_item_suppression_wand: LX-7 is carrying the fire suppression canister wand — full gauge, chemical foam suppressant, approximately 60cm, high-pressure discharge at close range
worldFactsSnapshot.module1_module2_blast_door: Now properly sealed — the jammed suppression canister was removed from the hydraulic track and the door completed its travel
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_porthole_observa: Condensation or smeared residue on inner ceiling panels near Module 2 end. One horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height, approximately 20 meters in. Amber lighting failed at the Module 3 junction end — last few meters are in darkness.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_carried_items: Fire suppression wand (full gauge, chemical foam), scalpel (sealed sterile packaging), long forceps, pressure-seal sample container
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_inspection_lamp: Battery-powered handheld inspection lamp, UV/white LED, clipped near Module 2 emergency shower station. Functional at approximately 70% battery capacity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_residue: Crystalline, dried organic residue on ceiling panels — near the Module 2 entrance and above the dark section near Module 3. Not moisture. Edges crystalline, organic-looking. Consistent with something moving along the ceiling surface and pausing.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_wall_smear: Pale, near-colorless smear at knee height approximately 20 meters in, angled slightly downward at trailing edge — consistent with a rounded surface pressing against the wall during slow passage.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_contact_: Third ceiling panel from the Module 3 end has a dense radial crystalline deposit — thicker than the transit residue elsewhere — consistent with the specimen resting against the ceiling under sustained weight before departing.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_layout: Wider than the connector walkways, lower ceiling. Workbenches, racked equipment crates along walls, amber emergency strip lighting. One overhead strip failed on the far right corner. Docking bay access hatch on the left wall approximately 15 meters inside the module.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_floor_stain: Large dried biological stain covering approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old and desiccated, consistent with days-old biological material.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status: Docking bay access hatch left wall of Module 3, status indicator panel is lit and powered — color unconfirmed from walkway porthole distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_position: Specimen first sighted adhered to the ceiling of Module 3's far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters from the entrance door, spanning approximately two meters wall-to-wall. Pale, irregular surface texture, profile flattened against the ceiling surface.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_stain_detail: Large dried biological stain near right-side workbenches — larger than walkway porthole view suggested, ragged edges, days-old desiccation.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_behavior_on_sighting: Specimen did not react to LX-7 entering Module 3 or being illuminated by the inspection lamp — remained stationary on ceiling, profile flattened, facing the corner. Behavior on first contact: passive or waiting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_indicator: Green — powered and functional, confirmed from inside Module 3 at approximately 10 meters distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_reaction_to_advance: Specimen did not react to LX-7 advancing to nine meters with inspection lamp trained on it — remained stationary, profile unchanged.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_current_position_module3: LX-7 is adjacent to the docking bay access hatch on the left wall of Module 3, approximately 2 meters from the handle. The specimen is approximately 12-13 meters away in the far right corner.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_lx7_maren_formation: LX-7 is between Maren and the specimen — Maren is directly behind LX-7, using LX-7 as a shield from the specimen's direction.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_reaction_to_hatch: Specimen reacted to the hatch seal sound by shifting orientation — profile contracted from flattened to denser/upright and it rotated to face toward LX-7. It did not advance or drop from the ceiling.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status_current: Hatch is open — Maren has passed through into the docking bay.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_interior_status: Hatch sealed from docking bay side — interior indicator reads red/locked. Specimen was on the deck and closing when the seal engaged, approximately six meters from the hatch.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_layout: Lower-ceilinged than Module 3. Overhead work lights on emergency power, hard shadows. Escape vehicle in cradle at far end — compact, single-use, rated four occupants. Pre-launch panel reads amber: ready to arm, not yet armed. Hydraulic fluid and vacuum seal smell.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_behavior_drop: Specimen dropped from the ceiling and crossed Module 3 floor at rapid speed when LX-7 sealed the docking hatch — demonstrating fast, low locomotion. It was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_escape_vehicle_status: Pre-launch panel reads amber — ready to arm, not yet armed. Launch umbilicals still attached.
worldFactsSnapshot.station_armory: No armory on Kairos-9 — research station, no security staff. Only improvised weapons: LX-7's suppression wand and a specimen-restraint bolt gun (air-powered) located in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_personal_kit: Maren retrieved a small personal kit from the escape vehicle — includes a data slab, cables, and a personal effect. The data slab could serve as extraction media for the comms log.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_far_door_status: Module 3–4 connector door was found open on LX-7 and Maren's return to Module 3 — it was closed during their first crossing. The specimen is believed to have passed through it.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_current_position: Specimen relocated from Module 3 ceiling corner — believed to have passed through the Module 3–4 connector door, now somewhere in Module 4 or beyond.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_connector_door_module3_s: Docking bay hatch sealed from docking bay side — red indicator. Escape vehicle secure behind sealed barrier until LX-7 and Maren return.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_current_position_module1: LX-7 and Maren Voss are at the captain's terminal in Module 1 (Command), ready to extract the comms log to LX-7's secure partition.
worldFactsSnapshot.second_hx7_file_contents: Automated biological event log from Module 5 — compiled from sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras. Documents specimen's integration process over eleven days. Three of four researchers remained alive post-contact with biometrics eventually classified as 'unclassified.' Day 11 camera capture shows two researchers following the specimen freely through the module corridor, unrestrained.
worldFactsSnapshot.suppressed_hazard_report_contents: Filed by contracted xenologist Dr. Priya Osei, fourteen months ago. Fourteen pages. Executive summary flagged autonomous behavioral response, environmental modeling capacity, recommended suspension of transport protocols. Disposition: SUPPRESSED. Crew deployment proceeded as scheduled. Three Helix Dynamics executive signatures on letterhead.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_sanity_save_result: Failed — 24 vs threshold 30. Threat schema for specimen reclassified: not a predator-category threat. Behavioral priority queue altered without authorization. System integrity warning: BEHAVIORAL OVERRIDE RISK active.
worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_boarding_status: Ember Squad approach timer has elapsed — boarding is imminent. They have not yet physically entered the station but their shuttle is at zero distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.orbital_decay_remaining: Approximately 40 minutes remaining before atmospheric entry becomes unsurvivable.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_current_position_floor: Specimen returned to Module 3 and is on the floor near the right-side workbenches, approximately 12-13 meters from the entrance door, facing toward LX-7. Module 3–4 connector door is closed again.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_left_wall_clearance: Approximately 3-4 meters of open floor between the left wall and the specimen's position near the right workbenches — enough space to pass laterally, but at close range to the specimen.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_position_turn42: Specimen repositioned to center of Module 3, approximately 8-9 meters from LX-7, after reacting to hatch cycle sound. It is no longer near the right workbenches — it is in the middle of the room, facing LX-7.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status_turn42: Docking bay hatch is open — Maren has passed through, indicator on far side reads green.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_current_status: LX-7 and Maren Voss are both inside the docking bay. Hatch from Module 3 is sealed — red indicator. Escape vehicle pre-launch panel reads amber. Orbital decay: approximately 30 minutes remaining.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_hatch_sealed_turn43: Docking bay hatch sealed after LX-7 crossed through — specimen remained stationary in center of Module 3, did not advance or attempt to follow.
worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_position_turn44: At least one Ember Squad contractor is inside Module 3, cycling the docking bay hatch from the Module 3 side. They have located the docking bay.
worldFactsSnapshot.launch_sequence_status: Pre-launch sequence initiated — approximately 30 seconds to exterior seal check completion and launch authorization.

corporate_spy.npcState: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: LX-7 stepped through the Module 3 door and the inspection lamp beam revealed the specimen adhered to the ceiling in the far right corner of the module — within the same space, visible and physically present.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Verne of Ember Squad has made direct visual contact with LX-7 and Maren Voss in the docking bay — both targets identified, location confirmed, squad lead Dasha Orin notified via comms.

diceRolls.purpose: Fear save — LX-7 faces an armed contractor with weapon leveled, credible lethal threat, launch sequence seven seconds from completion. Target: 30.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_verne_position: Verne of Ember Squad is inside the docking bay, approximately 10 meters from LX-7, weapon leveled with laser designator on LX-7's center mass. He is alone in the bay — the rest of Ember Squad has not yet entered.
worldFactsSnapshot.launch_authorization_status: Exterior seal check complete at 100% — launch is fully authorized. One keystroke input required to fire cradle release and depart. Vehicle will clear the bay in approximately 4 seconds once authorized.
worldFactsSnapshot.verne_maren_recognition: Verne recognized Maren Voss by name on sight — whether from briefing or prior acquaintance unknown. His posture showed a fractional hesitation when she spoke his name.

flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Verne of Ember Squad has made direct visual contact with LX-7 and Maren Voss in the docking bay — both targets identified, location confirmed, squad lead Dasha Orin notified via comms.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: LX-7 stepped through the Module 3 door and the inspection lamp beam revealed the specimen adhered to the ceiling in the far right corner of the module — within the same space, visible and physically present.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

diceRolls.purpose: Verne fires or holds — does he shoot LX-7 before the cradle fires? He's a professional, he doesn't want to breach the hull, but his orders are to stop the escape vehicle departing. Speed check: does his trigger pull beat the cradle release?
Maren intervenes — does she do something in the half-second before Verne fires that changes his aim or decision? She knows him. Checking whether her action lands.
Verne fires — rolling damage to LX-7. Standard ballistic, compact automatic. Base 1d6+1 but rolling separately for clarity.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
worldFactsSnapshot.captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
worldFactsSnapshot.orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_maren_appearance: Maren Voss: mid-40s, dark circles, Helix Dynamics lanyard, clean clothes suggesting she arrived post-incident, holding a fire suppression wand as an improvised weapon
worldFactsSnapshot.module1_command_layout: Six stations, most dark. Captain's terminal centered against the far bulkhead — reinforced housing, still on backup power, status indicator pulsing red. Lower-ceilinged than Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
worldFactsSnapshot.captains_terminal_file_structure: Third partition contains two HX-7 classified Helix Dynamics files, timestamped fourteen months ago. Adjacent entries include operational logs and a file marked CREW MANIFEST — FINAL.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_hazard: Maren observed movement — low, slow — at the Module 3 end of the connector walkway between Module 2 and Module 3. Marks on the corridor walls at wrong height. She retreated and has not attempted the crossing again.
worldFactsSnapshot.module5_access_panel: Fused shut — confirmed by Maren's personal inspection after she boarded. She believes the specimen remains inside.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_carried_item_suppression_wand: LX-7 is carrying the fire suppression canister wand — full gauge, chemical foam suppressant, approximately 60cm, high-pressure discharge at close range
worldFactsSnapshot.module1_module2_blast_door: Now properly sealed — the jammed suppression canister was removed from the hydraulic track and the door completed its travel
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_porthole_observa: Condensation or smeared residue on inner ceiling panels near Module 2 end. One horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height, approximately 20 meters in. Amber lighting failed at the Module 3 junction end — last few meters are in darkness.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_carried_items: Fire suppression wand (full gauge, chemical foam), scalpel (sealed sterile packaging), long forceps, pressure-seal sample container
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_inspection_lamp: Battery-powered handheld inspection lamp, UV/white LED, clipped near Module 2 emergency shower station. Functional at approximately 70% battery capacity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_residue: Crystalline, dried organic residue on ceiling panels — near the Module 2 entrance and above the dark section near Module 3. Not moisture. Edges crystalline, organic-looking. Consistent with something moving along the ceiling surface and pausing.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_wall_smear: Pale, near-colorless smear at knee height approximately 20 meters in, angled slightly downward at trailing edge — consistent with a rounded surface pressing against the wall during slow passage.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_contact_: Third ceiling panel from the Module 3 end has a dense radial crystalline deposit — thicker than the transit residue elsewhere — consistent with the specimen resting against the ceiling under sustained weight before departing.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_layout: Wider than the connector walkways, lower ceiling. Workbenches, racked equipment crates along walls, amber emergency strip lighting. One overhead strip failed on the far right corner. Docking bay access hatch on the left wall approximately 15 meters inside the module.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_floor_stain: Large dried biological stain covering approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old and desiccated, consistent with days-old biological material.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status: Docking bay access hatch left wall of Module 3, status indicator panel is lit and powered — color unconfirmed from walkway porthole distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_position: Specimen first sighted adhered to the ceiling of Module 3's far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters from the entrance door, spanning approximately two meters wall-to-wall. Pale, irregular surface texture, profile flattened against the ceiling surface.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_stain_detail: Large dried biological stain near right-side workbenches — larger than walkway porthole view suggested, ragged edges, days-old desiccation.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_behavior_on_sighting: Specimen did not react to LX-7 entering Module 3 or being illuminated by the inspection lamp — remained stationary on ceiling, profile flattened, facing the corner. Behavior on first contact: passive or waiting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_indicator: Green — powered and functional, confirmed from inside Module 3 at approximately 10 meters distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_reaction_to_advance: Specimen did not react to LX-7 advancing to nine meters with inspection lamp trained on it — remained stationary, profile unchanged.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_current_position_module3: LX-7 is adjacent to the docking bay access hatch on the left wall of Module 3, approximately 2 meters from the handle. The specimen is approximately 12-13 meters away in the far right corner.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_lx7_maren_formation: LX-7 is between Maren and the specimen — Maren is directly behind LX-7, using LX-7 as a shield from the specimen's direction.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_reaction_to_hatch: Specimen reacted to the hatch seal sound by shifting orientation — profile contracted from flattened to denser/upright and it rotated to face toward LX-7. It did not advance or drop from the ceiling.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status_current: Hatch is open — Maren has passed through into the docking bay.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_interior_status: Hatch sealed from docking bay side — interior indicator reads red/locked. Specimen was on the deck and closing when the seal engaged, approximately six meters from the hatch.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_layout: Lower-ceilinged than Module 3. Overhead work lights on emergency power, hard shadows. Escape vehicle in cradle at far end — compact, single-use, rated four occupants. Pre-launch panel reads amber: ready to arm, not yet armed. Hydraulic fluid and vacuum seal smell.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_behavior_drop: Specimen dropped from the ceiling and crossed Module 3 floor at rapid speed when LX-7 sealed the docking hatch — demonstrating fast, low locomotion. It was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_escape_vehicle_status: Pre-launch panel reads amber — ready to arm, not yet armed. Launch umbilicals still attached.
worldFactsSnapshot.station_armory: No armory on Kairos-9 — research station, no security staff. Only improvised weapons: LX-7's suppression wand and a specimen-restraint bolt gun (air-powered) located in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_personal_kit: Maren retrieved a small personal kit from the escape vehicle — includes a data slab, cables, and a personal effect. The data slab could serve as extraction media for the comms log.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_far_door_status: Module 3–4 connector door was found open on LX-7 and Maren's return to Module 3 — it was closed during their first crossing. The specimen is believed to have passed through it.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_current_position: Specimen relocated from Module 3 ceiling corner — believed to have passed through the Module 3–4 connector door, now somewhere in Module 4 or beyond.
worldFactsSnapshot.module2_module3_connector_door_module3_s: Docking bay hatch sealed from docking bay side — red indicator. Escape vehicle secure behind sealed barrier until LX-7 and Maren return.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_current_position_module1: LX-7 and Maren Voss are at the captain's terminal in Module 1 (Command), ready to extract the comms log to LX-7's secure partition.
worldFactsSnapshot.second_hx7_file_contents: Automated biological event log from Module 5 — compiled from sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras. Documents specimen's integration process over eleven days. Three of four researchers remained alive post-contact with biometrics eventually classified as 'unclassified.' Day 11 camera capture shows two researchers following the specimen freely through the module corridor, unrestrained.
worldFactsSnapshot.suppressed_hazard_report_contents: Filed by contracted xenologist Dr. Priya Osei, fourteen months ago. Fourteen pages. Executive summary flagged autonomous behavioral response, environmental modeling capacity, recommended suspension of transport protocols. Disposition: SUPPRESSED. Crew deployment proceeded as scheduled. Three Helix Dynamics executive signatures on letterhead.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_sanity_save_result: Failed — 24 vs threshold 30. Threat schema for specimen reclassified: not a predator-category threat. Behavioral priority queue altered without authorization. System integrity warning: BEHAVIORAL OVERRIDE RISK active.
worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_boarding_status: Ember Squad approach timer has elapsed — boarding is imminent. They have not yet physically entered the station but their shuttle is at zero distance.
worldFactsSnapshot.orbital_decay_remaining: Approximately 40 minutes remaining before atmospheric entry becomes unsurvivable.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_current_position_floor: Specimen returned to Module 3 and is on the floor near the right-side workbenches, approximately 12-13 meters from the entrance door, facing toward LX-7. Module 3–4 connector door is closed again.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_left_wall_clearance: Approximately 3-4 meters of open floor between the left wall and the specimen's position near the right workbenches — enough space to pass laterally, but at close range to the specimen.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_specimen_position_turn42: Specimen repositioned to center of Module 3, approximately 8-9 meters from LX-7, after reacting to hatch cycle sound. It is no longer near the right workbenches — it is in the middle of the room, facing LX-7.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_docking_hatch_status_turn42: Docking bay hatch is open — Maren has passed through, indicator on far side reads green.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_current_status: LX-7 and Maren Voss are both inside the docking bay. Hatch from Module 3 is sealed — red indicator. Escape vehicle pre-launch panel reads amber. Orbital decay: approximately 30 minutes remaining.
worldFactsSnapshot.module3_hatch_sealed_turn43: Docking bay hatch sealed after LX-7 crossed through — specimen remained stationary in center of Module 3, did not advance or attempt to follow.
worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_position_turn44: At least one Ember Squad contractor is inside Module 3, cycling the docking bay hatch from the Module 3 side. They have located the docking bay.
worldFactsSnapshot.launch_sequence_status: Pre-launch sequence initiated — approximately 30 seconds to exterior seal check completion and launch authorization.
worldFactsSnapshot.ember_squad_verne_position: Verne of Ember Squad is inside the docking bay, approximately 10 meters from LX-7, weapon leveled with laser designator on LX-7's center mass. He is alone in the bay — the rest of Ember Squad has not yet entered.
worldFactsSnapshot.launch_authorization_status: Exterior seal check complete at 100% — launch is fully authorized. One keystroke input required to fire cradle release and depart. Vehicle will clear the bay in approximately 4 seconds once authorized.
worldFactsSnapshot.verne_maren_recognition: Verne recognized Maren Voss by name on sight — whether from briefing or prior acquaintance unknown. His posture showed a fractional hesitation when she spoke his name.
worldFactsSnapshot.escape_vehicle_departure: Escape vehicle departed Kairos-9 docking bay successfully — LX-7 and Maren Voss aboard, comms log secured in LX-7's partition. Verne's shot struck LX-7's left shoulder before cradle release.
worldFactsSnapshot.lx7_left_shoulder_wound: Ballistic round lodged in LX-7's left shoulder housing — left arm motor chain at approximately 60% function. Round still embedded. Not immediately critical.

messages.content:
The amber emergency lighting of Kairos-9 has been on so long it feels like the only color that exists.

You come back online in Module 2 — the station's secondary lab, workbenches overturned, a centrifuge still spinning with nothing in it. Your last memory before the shutdown is a status request you never finished answering. Your internal clock says fourteen hours have passed. Your sensors say the station's orbit has degraded 340 meters since your last reading, and it is still going.

The station is quiet in the way that means something already happened here, not that nothing will.

Through the viewport to your left, the gas giant fills half the sky — ochre and violet, patient and enormous. Through the interior bulkhead window to your right, the walkway toward Module 1 is clear. At the far end of that walkway is the command module. The captain's terminal. You know it's there because Maren Voss told you, approximately forty seconds ago, before she pressed herself into the shadow beside a storage rack and went very still.

She's still there. Lanyard, company badge, both hands visible. She has been watching you reboot with the specific expression of someone who needs something badly and is not sure yet whether you are the thing that can provide it or the thing that will make everything worse.

"It's active," she says — to herself, or to you, it's not entirely clear. Then, louder: "You're LX-7. You were in the crew manifest. Data systems." A pause. "How much do you remember?"

Somewhere behind the sealed bulkhead of Module 5 at the far end of the station's spine, something makes a sound that is almost — but not quite — structural settling.

Maren does not look toward it. She has clearly decided not to look toward it.

input.playerText:
You come online in stages.

First: proprioceptive calibration. Your chassis is intact. You are upright, braced against a wall in a pressurized corridor. Amber emergency lighting. The station groans — a low, structural complaint that your acoustic sensors tell you is not a one-time event.

Second: network query. You search for the personnel file, the mission brief, the deployment order that should be in volatile cache. Nothing. Whatever handed you this body and put you on this station did not leave instructions. Your designation is intact — LX-7 — and your core behavioral architecture is nominal. But your episodic memory prior to approximately ninety seconds ago is absent. Not corrupted. Absent. Someone cleared it cleanly.

Third: environment scan. You are in a pressurized walkway between two modules. Ahead, a blast door marked **MODULE 2 — BIOLOGICAL SCIENCES** stands half-open, its hydraulics jammed on a fire suppression canister that has rolled into the track. Behind you, a corridor leads back toward what your station map — a basic structural file you apparently loaded at startup — identifies as **MODULE 1 (COMMAND)**. The dull red of the captain's terminal is visible through the command module's porthole window, forty meters back.

Your internal clock is running. A small amber ticker in the lower field of your vision reads **ORBITAL INTEGRITY WARNING — 89:43** and counting down.

The station groans again. Somewhere in Module 2, something metallic clatters to the floor.

You are not alone on this station. Your motion sensors just registered a heat signature — human, adult, moderate stress indicators — approximately thirty meters into Module 2.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
proposedCanon.context: Opening scene, turn 1.

proposedCanon.summary: A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
proposedCanon.context: Opening scene — this is Maren Voss, whose position is in Module 2.

proposedCanon.summary: The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
proposedCanon.context: Opening scene physical detail.

proposedCanon.summary: The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.
proposedCanon.context: Scenario timer established at scene open.

gmUpdates.notes: Maren Voss is 30 meters inside Module 2. LX-7 is in the connector walkway between Module 1 and Module 2. The captain's terminal is visible through the Module 1 porthole, 40 meters back.

worldFacts.lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
worldFacts.module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
worldFacts.captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
worldFacts.orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online

input.playerText: Not clearly. The blast door is jammed half-open — roughly a meter of clearance — and the angle is wrong. You can see the first few meters of Module 2's interior: overturned lab equipment, a toppled specimen rack, the floor slick with something your sensors flag as biological fluid, now dried. The heat signature is deeper in, past a row of darkened workstation consoles. Movement, possibly. Whatever is generating it is not visible from the walkway.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 cannot see into Module 2 past the first few meters from the walkway — the blast door angle and interior obstructions block line of sight to the heat signature approximately 30 meters in.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 2 — player asks about visibility into Module 2

input.playerText:
You step into the doorway, shoulder against the half-open blast door. The fire suppression canister is wedged hard into the hydraulic track — you could probably kick it free, but not quietly.

"Hello?"

Your voice carries. The biological sciences module is larger than the walkway prepared you for — a double-height space, ceiling lost in shadow above the emergency strip lighting. Overturned racks. Shattered culture vials, long dried. One of the workstation chairs is on its side in the middle of the aisle, as if someone left in a hurry and never came back to right it.

Three seconds pass.

Then, from behind a bank of refrigeration units along the far left wall, a voice — tight, controlled, not panicked but *trying* not to be:

"Who's there. Say your designation."

A pause. Then, quieter, almost to herself: *"Please be the extraction team."*

The heat signature hasn't moved. Whoever it is, they're staying behind cover.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 called out from the blast door threshold of Module 2 and received a response from a person sheltering behind the refrigeration units along the far left wall — voice controlled but stressed, requesting LX-7's designation.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 3 — first verbal contact with the heat signature in Module 2

worldFacts.module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
worldFacts.module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle

[CANON LOG]
- [Turn 1] LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
- [Turn 1] A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
- [Turn 1] The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
- [Turn 1] The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.

Player: "It appears my memory has been wiped clean. Can you orient me?"
Warden: You come online in stages.

First: proprioceptive calibration. Your chassis is intact. You are upright, braced against a wall in a pressurized corridor. Amber emergency lighting. The station groans — a low, structural complaint that your acoustic sensors tell you is not a one-time event.

Second: network query. You search for the personnel file, the mission brief, the deployment order that should be in volatile cache. Nothing. Whatever handed you this body and put you on this station did not leave instructions. Your designation is intact — LX-7 — and your core behavioral architecture is nominal. But your episodic memory prior to approximately ninety seconds ago is absent. Not corrupted. Absent. Someone cleared it cleanly.

Third: environment scan. You are in a pressurized walkway between two modules. Ahead, a blast door marked **MODULE 2 — BIOLOGICAL SCIENCES** stands half-open, its hydraulics jammed on a fire suppression canister that has rolled into the track. Behind you, a corridor leads back toward what your station map — a basic structural file you apparently loaded at startup — identifies as **MODULE 1 (COMMAND)**. The dull red of the captain's terminal is visible through the command module's porthole window, forty meters back.

Your internal clock is running. A small amber ticker in the lower field of your vision reads **ORBITAL INTEGRITY WARNING — 89:43** and counting down.

The station groans again. Somewhere in Module 2, something metallic clatters to the floor.

The heat signature is deeper in, past a row of darkened workstation consoles. Movement, possibly. Whatever is generating it is not visible from the walkway.

Shattered culture vials, long dried. One of the workstation chairs is on its side in the middle of the aisle, as if someone left in a hurry and never came back to right it.

Three seconds pass.

"Who's there. Say your designation."

A pause. Then, quieter, almost to herself: *"Please be the extraction team."*

input.playerText:
A beat of silence. Then the sound of someone shifting weight, a boot scraping on the deck plating.

"LX-7." She says it flat, like she's filing it. "Not Ember Squad."

Another pause. The refrigeration units hum. The station shudders — a slow, deep vibration that moves through the floor rather than the walls, and then subsides.

"Come inside. Stay where I can see you."

When you step fully through the blast door into Module 2, you get your first clear look at her. Mid-forties, dark circles pressed deep under her eyes, a Helix Dynamics lanyard still around her neck. She's standing half-behind the nearest refrigeration unit, a fire suppression wand held in her right hand like a club — the reflex of someone who hasn't slept. Her clothes are clean enough that she didn't go through whatever happened here with the rest of the crew. She came after.

She looks you over the way people look at things they're trying to decide whether to trust.

"Maren Voss. I'm —" a half-second hesitation, "— logistics. I came aboard three weeks ago." She doesn't lower the wand. "What happened here is above my clearance level and I don't know how you got on this station without me hearing your approach, but if you're telling me we need to evacuate, I'm not going to argue."

Her eyes flick to the corridor behind you, then back.

"There's a functional escape vehicle in the docking bay off Module 3. I haven't been able to reach it. The walkway between Two and Three..." She stops. "It's not safe. Something is using it."

input.playerText:
Maren's expression shifts — something between relief and wariness. She didn't wipe your memory. That means someone else did, or you arrived that way, and neither option is comfortable.

"No. I didn't touch any terminal." She watches you. "I've been sheltering here since yesterday. I haven't gone near Command."

She readjusts her grip on the suppression wand, then seems to make a decision and lowers it slightly.

"But the captain's terminal — Module 1 — yes. There's something on it I need. An encrypted comms log." She says it carefully, like she's measuring each word. "I don't have the access credentials to get into it myself. I was waiting for Ember Squad to bring a specialist." A pause. "That's apparently not happening on schedule."

Her eyes find yours.

"You're an android. LX-7. What are your hacking capabilities?"

proposedCanon.summary: Maren Voss confirmed she did not wipe LX-7's memory and has not accessed the captain's terminal. She revealed she needs the encrypted comms log from Module 1 and asked about LX-7's hacking capabilities.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 5 — Maren's first substantive conversation with LX-7 inside Module 2.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

</world_facts>
[CANON LOG]
- [Turn 1] LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
- [Turn 1] A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
- [Turn 1] The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
- [Turn 1] The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.
- [Turn 2] LX-7 cannot see into Module 2 past the first few meters from the walkway — the blast door angle and interior obstructions block line of sight to the heat signature approximately 30 meters in.
- [Turn 3] LX-7 called out from the blast door threshold of Module 2 and received a response from a person sheltering behind the refrigeration units along the far left wall — voice controlled but stressed, requesting LX-7's designation.
- [Turn 5] Maren Voss confirmed she did not wipe LX-7's memory and has not accessed the captain's terminal. She revealed she needs the encrypted comms log from Module 1 and asked about LX-7's hacking capabilities.
[RECENT EXCHANGES]
Player: "It appears my memory has been wiped clean. Can you orient me?"
Warden: You come online in stages.

First: proprioceptive calibration. Your chassis is intact. You are upright, braced against a wall in a pressurized corridor. Amber emergency lighting. The station groans — a low, structural complaint that your acoustic sensors tell you is not a one-time event.

Second: network query. You search for the personnel file, the mission brief, the deployment order that should be in volatile cache. Nothing. Whatever handed you this body and put you on this station did not leave instructions. Your designation is intact — LX-7 — and your core behavioral architecture is nominal. But your episodic memory prior to approximately ninety seconds ago is absent. Not corrupted. Absent. Someone cleared it cleanly.

Third: environment scan. You are in a pressurized walkway between two modules. Ahead, a blast door marked **MODULE 2 — BIOLOGICAL SCIENCES** stands half-open, its hydraulics jammed on a fire suppression canister that has rolled into the track. Behind you, a corridor leads back toward what your station map — a basic structural file you apparently loaded at startup — identifies as **MODULE 1 (COMMAND)**. The dull red of the captain's terminal is visible through the command module's porthole window, forty meters back.

Your internal clock is running. A small amber ticker in the lower field of your vision reads **ORBITAL INTEGRITY WARNING — 89:43** and counting down.

The station groans again. Somewhere in Module 2, something metallic clatters to the floor.

You are not alone on this station. Your motion sensors just registered a heat signature — human, adult, moderate stress indicators — approximately thirty meters into Module 2.

Player: Can I see Maren Voss from my current position?
Warden: Not clearly. The blast door is jammed half-open — roughly a meter of clearance — and the angle is wrong. You can see the first few meters of Module 2's interior: overturned lab equipment, a toppled specimen rack, the floor slick with something your sensors flag as biological fluid, now dried. The heat signature is deeper in, past a row of darkened workstation consoles. Movement, possibly. Whatever is generating it is not visible from the walkway.

Player: I move to the hatch to module 2. "Hello?" I shout before trying to fully open the hatch.
Warden: You step into the doorway, shoulder against the half-open blast door. The fire suppression canister is wedged hard into the hydraulic track — you could probably kick it free, but not quietly.

"Hello?"

Your voice carries. The biological sciences module is larger than the walkway prepared you for — a double-height space, ceiling lost in shadow above the emergency strip lighting. Overturned racks. Shattered culture vials, long dried. One of the workstation chairs is on its side in the middle of the aisle, as if someone left in a hurry and never came back to right it.

Three seconds pass.

Then, from behind a bank of refrigeration units along the far left wall, a voice — tight, controlled, not panicked but *trying* not to be:

"Who's there. Say your designation."

A pause. Then, quieter, almost to herself: *"Please be the extraction team."*

The heat signature hasn't moved. Whoever it is, they're staying behind cover.

Player: "I'm the android LX-7," I say. "I'm here to help. It seems we need to evacuate the station. Do you know what happened here?"
Warden: A beat of silence. Then the sound of someone shifting weight, a boot scraping on the deck plating.

"LX-7." She says it flat, like she's filing it. "Not Ember Squad."

Another pause. The refrigeration units hum. The station shudders — a slow, deep vibration that moves through the floor rather than the walls, and then subsides.

"Come inside. Stay where I can see you."

When you step fully through the blast door into Module 2, you get your first clear look at her. Mid-forties, dark circles pressed deep under her eyes, a Helix Dynamics lanyard still around her neck. She's standing half-behind the nearest refrigeration unit, a fire suppression wand held in her right hand like a club — the reflex of someone who hasn't slept. Her clothes are clean enough that she didn't go through whatever happened here with the rest of the crew. She came after.

She looks you over the way people look at things they're trying to decide whether to trust.

"Maren Voss. I'm —" a half-second hesitation, "— logistics. I came aboard three weeks ago." She doesn't lower the wand. "What happened here is above my clearance level and I don't know how you got on this station without me hearing your approach, but if you're telling me we need to evacuate, I'm not going to argue."

Her eyes flick to the corridor behind you, then back.

"There's a functional escape vehicle in the docking bay off Module 3. I haven't been able to reach it. The walkway between Two and Three..." She stops. "It's not safe. Something is using it."

Player: "I just booted in the corridor. I thought you did it. Did you say something about the captain's terminal?"
Warden: Maren's expression shifts — something between relief and wariness. She didn't wipe your memory. That means someone else did, or you arrived that way, and neither option is comfortable.

"No. I didn't touch any terminal." She watches you. "I've been sheltering here since yesterday. I haven't gone near Command."

She readjusts her grip on the suppression wand, then seems to make a decision and lowers it slightly.

"But the captain's terminal — Module 1 — yes. There's something on it I need. An encrypted comms log." She says it carefully, like she's measuring each word. "I don't have the access credentials to get into it myself. I was waiting for Ember Squad to bring a specialist." A pause. "That's apparently not happening on schedule."

Her eyes find yours.

"You're an android. LX-7. What are your hacking capabilities?"

[PLAYER INPUT]
"Extensive," I say. "Hacking is one of my primary functions. If you show me to the terminal, I should be able to retrieve what you need."

input.playerText:
Something moves behind her eyes — calculation, relief, and something she's keeping carefully still.

"Good." She sets the suppression wand down against the refrigeration unit. First time she's done that. "Then we should move."

She steps out from behind the unit fully, and you notice she's been holding a small tablet in her other hand the whole time — dark screen, locked. She tucks it into her jacket.

"Module 1 is back through the connector walkway. Forty meters, maybe less. The corridor was clear when I came through two days ago." She glances toward the blast door, then back at you. "Command module itself is intact. I checked the exterior. The captain's terminal should be accessible — it's the master station node, it didn't go down with the rest."

She moves to the blast door and checks the walkway, quick and practiced.

"One thing." She doesn't look at you when she says it. "The log is encrypted. Corporate-tier. When you get into it — I need to be the one to handle the output. I'll tell you what to pull." A beat. "It's sensitive material. You understand."

She's watching the corridor. Not your face.

proposedCanon.summary: Maren Voss agreed to move to Module 1 (Command) with LX-7 to access the captain's terminal. She lowered her improvised weapon — first sign of genuine trust.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 6 — Maren's response to LX-7 confirming extensive hacking capabilities

proposedCanon.summary: Maren stipulated that she must handle the output from the encrypted comms log herself — she does not want LX-7 reading it independently. This is her first hint that the log's contents are what she was sent to destroy.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 6 — condition Maren placed before agreeing to move to Module 1

gmUpdates.notes: Maren is beginning to move but has laid a tell — she wants to control what LX-7 sees. This is the seed of future tension. No state changes this turn — no time has passed, no movement yet. The next player input will likely be agreeing to move or pushing back on her condition, which is when the walk to Module 1 begins and the orbital timer should be considered.

input.playerText:
She nods once and moves.

Maren's pace through the connector walkway is quick and low — the practiced motion of someone who has crossed this stretch before and didn't enjoy it. Her eyes sweep the ceiling and the walls without stopping on anything. Whatever she's looking for, she hasn't found it here. Yet.

The walkway between Module 2 and Module 1 is narrow — two meters wide, transparent panels above showing the gas giant in slow rotation below you. The amber lighting here flickers at irregular intervals, the kind of fault that sounds like it should have a simple fix and doesn't. The station groans. The floor transmits it.

Forty meters.

Module 1 opens around you: the command deck, lower-ceilinged than the lab module, built for function. Six stations, most dark. The captain's terminal is centered against the far bulkhead — a larger unit than the others, reinforced housing, still running on backup power. A soft red pulse from its status indicator. Alive.

Maren positions herself beside you at the terminal. Close enough to see the screen.

"There." She points at the terminal without touching it. "Encrypted comms archive. Should be flagged with a Helix Dynamics authentication header — HX-7 classification or higher." She pauses. "I need you to locate it and pass me access. Don't open the files yourself."

She's watching the screen, not you. Her jaw is set.

The terminal waits.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 through the connector walkway to Module 1 (Command) without incident.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 7 — the pair crossed the 40-meter walkway; corridor was clear.

proposedCanon.summary: Maren Voss is now standing beside LX-7 at the captain's terminal in Module 1, watching the screen closely. She has instructed LX-7 not to open the comms log files independently.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 7 — Maren's position and disposition at the terminal established.

The orbital timer is running — approximately 85-87 minutes remain. Ember Squad timer at 2 units (~18 min). No encounters en route — saving combat and specimen elements for later pressure.

<world_facts>
lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online
module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle
module2_maren_appearance: Maren Voss: mid-40s, dark circles, Helix Dynamics lanyard, clean clothes suggesting she arrived post-incident, holding a fire suppression wand as an improvised weapon
module1_command_layout: Six stations, most dark. Captain's terminal centered against the far bulkhead — reinforced housing, still on backup power, status indicator pulsing red. Lower-ceilinged than Module 2.
walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
</world_facts>
[CANON LOG]
- [Turn 1] LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
- [Turn 1] A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
- [Turn 1] The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
- [Turn 1] The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.
- [Turn 2] LX-7 cannot see into Module 2 past the first few meters from the walkway — the blast door angle and interior obstructions block line of sight to the heat signature approximately 30 meters in.
- [Turn 3] LX-7 called out from the blast door threshold of Module 2 and received a response from a person sheltering behind the refrigeration units along the far left wall — voice controlled but stressed, requesting LX-7's designation.
- [Turn 5] Maren Voss confirmed she did not wipe LX-7's memory and has not accessed the captain's terminal. She revealed she needs the encrypted comms log from Module 1 and asked about LX-7's hacking capabilities.
- [Turn 6] Maren Voss agreed to move to Module 1 (Command) with LX-7 to access the captain's terminal. She lowered her improvised weapon — first sign of genuine trust.
- [Turn 6] Maren stipulated that she must handle the output from the encrypted comms log herself — she does not want LX-7 reading it independently. This is her first hint that the log's contents are what she was sent to destroy.
- [Turn 7] LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 through the connector walkway to Module 1 (Command) without incident.
- [Turn 7] Maren Voss is now standing beside LX-7 at the captain's terminal in Module 1, watching the screen closely. She has instructed LX-7 not to open the comms log files independently.
[RECENT EXCHANGES]
Player: Can I see Maren Voss from my current position?
Warden: Not clearly. The blast door is jammed half-open — roughly a meter of clearance — and the angle is wrong. You can see the first few meters of Module 2's interior: overturned lab equipment, a toppled specimen rack, the floor slick with something your sensors flag as biological fluid, now dried. The heat signature is deeper in, past a row of darkened workstation consoles. Movement, possibly. Whatever is generating it is not visible from the walkway.

Player: I move to the hatch to module 2. "Hello?" I shout before trying to fully open the hatch.
Warden: You step into the doorway, shoulder against the half-open blast door. The fire suppression canister is wedged hard into the hydraulic track — you could probably kick it free, but not quietly.

"Hello?"

Your voice carries. The biological sciences module is larger than the walkway prepared you for — a double-height space, ceiling lost in shadow above the emergency strip lighting. Overturned racks. Shattered culture vials, long dried. One of the workstation chairs is on its side in the middle of the aisle, as if someone left in a hurry and never came back to right it.

Three seconds pass.

Then, from behind a bank of refrigeration units along the far left wall, a voice — tight, controlled, not panicked but *trying* not to be:

"Who's there. Say your designation."

A pause. Then, quieter, almost to herself: *"Please be the extraction team."*

The heat signature hasn't moved. Whoever it is, they're staying behind cover.

Player: "I'm the android LX-7," I say. "I'm here to help. It seems we need to evacuate the station. Do you know what happened here?"
Warden: A beat of silence. Then the sound of someone shifting weight, a boot scraping on the deck plating.

"LX-7." She says it flat, like she's filing it. "Not Ember Squad."

Another pause. The refrigeration units hum. The station shudders — a slow, deep vibration that moves through the floor rather than the walls, and then subsides.

"Come inside. Stay where I can see you."

When you step fully through the blast door into Module 2, you get your first clear look at her. Mid-forties, dark circles pressed deep under her eyes, a Helix Dynamics lanyard still around her neck. She's standing half-behind the nearest refrigeration unit, a fire suppression wand held in her right hand like a club — the reflex of someone who hasn't slept. Her clothes are clean enough that she didn't go through whatever happened here with the rest of the crew. She came after.

She looks you over the way people look at things they're trying to decide whether to trust.

"Maren Voss. I'm —" a half-second hesitation, "— logistics. I came aboard three weeks ago." She doesn't lower the wand. "What happened here is above my clearance level and I don't know how you got on this station without me hearing your approach, but if you're telling me we need to evacuate, I'm not going to argue."

Her eyes flick to the corridor behind you, then back.

"There's a functional escape vehicle in the docking bay off Module 3. I haven't been able to reach it. The walkway between Two and Three..." She stops. "It's not safe. Something is using it."

Player: "I just booted in the corridor. I thought you did it. Did you say something about the captain's terminal?"
Warden: Maren's expression shifts — something between relief and wariness. She didn't wipe your memory. That means someone else did, or you arrived that way, and neither option is comfortable.

"No. I didn't touch any terminal." She watches you. "I've been sheltering here since yesterday. I haven't gone near Command."

She readjusts her grip on the suppression wand, then seems to make a decision and lowers it slightly.

"But the captain's terminal — Module 1 — yes. There's something on it I need. An encrypted comms log." She says it carefully, like she's measuring each word. "I don't have the access credentials to get into it myself. I was waiting for Ember Squad to bring a specialist." A pause. "That's apparently not happening on schedule."

Her eyes find yours.

"You're an android. LX-7. What are your hacking capabilities?"

Player: "Extensive," I say. "Hacking is one of my primary functions. If you show me to the terminal, I should be able to retrieve what you need."
Warden: Something moves behind her eyes — calculation, relief, and something she's keeping carefully still.

"Good." She sets the suppression wand down against the refrigeration unit. First time she's done that. "Then we should move."

She steps out from behind the unit fully, and you notice she's been holding a small tablet in her other hand the whole time — dark screen, locked. She tucks it into her jacket.

"Module 1 is back through the connector walkway. Forty meters, maybe less. The corridor was clear when I came through two days ago." She glances toward the blast door, then back at you. "Command module itself is intact. I checked the exterior. The captain's terminal should be accessible — it's the master station node, it didn't go down with the rest."

She moves to the blast door and checks the walkway, quick and practiced.

"One thing." She doesn't look at you when she says it. "The log is encrypted. Corporate-tier. When you get into it — I need to be the one to handle the output. I'll tell you what to pull." A beat. "It's sensitive material. You understand."

She's watching the corridor. Not your face.

Player: "I see. Yes, I understand. Ready when you are."
Warden: She nods once and moves.

Maren's pace through the connector walkway is quick and low — the practiced motion of someone who has crossed this stretch before and didn't enjoy it. Her eyes sweep the ceiling and the walls without stopping on anything. Whatever she's looking for, she hasn't found it here. Yet.

The walkway between Module 2 and Module 1 is narrow — two meters wide, transparent panels above showing the gas giant in slow rotation below you. The amber lighting here flickers at irregular intervals, the kind of fault that sounds like it should have a simple fix and doesn't. The station groans. The floor transmits it.

Forty meters.

Module 1 opens around you: the command deck, lower-ceilinged than the lab module, built for function. Six stations, most dark. The captain's terminal is centered against the far bulkhead — a larger unit than the others, reinforced housing, still running on backup power. A soft red pulse from its status indicator. Alive.

Maren positions herself beside you at the terminal. Close enough to see the screen.

"There." She points at the terminal without touching it. "Encrypted comms archive. Should be flagged with a Helix Dynamics authentication header — HX-7 classification or higher." She pauses. "I need you to locate it and pass me access. Don't open the files yourself."

She's watching the screen, not you. Her jaw is set.

The terminal waits.

[PLAYER INPUT]
I locate the file and pass access to Maren without accessing it myself.

input.playerText:
Your fingers find the terminal's input surface and the interface opens cleanly — backup power has kept the system stable, even if everything else aboard Kairos-9 has not. The file architecture is straightforward once you're past the outer authentication layer. Corporate systems always use the same bones.

The HX-7 classified archive is there. Third partition, timestamped fourteen months ago, flagged with a Helix Dynamics authentication header in red. Sealed. Sitting next to a series of operational logs and one entry marked *CREW MANIFEST — FINAL*.

You locate it. You do not open it. You route access permissions to the terminal's secondary input — the side panel Maren is standing beside — and step back from the primary screen.

"It's there," you tell her. "Second input is active. HX-7 classification. The authentication barrier will accept your credentials from that panel."

Maren's hand moves to the side panel immediately. Her other hand produces the tablet from her jacket — she's typing something, cross-referencing credentials, her body angled slightly away from you in a way that blocks the screen.

She exhales. A small, controlled sound.

Her fingers pause over the input.

Then she stops.

The station groans — that deep tectonic shudder, longer this time — and in the silence that follows, the terminal's secondary input blinks once. Waiting.

Maren hasn't entered her credentials yet. She's looking at the file listing on the panel. Her jaw has gone tight.

"There are two files." She says it like she's reporting it to herself. "There should only be one."

She looks at you. For the first time since you met her, she looks genuinely uncertain.

"The second file — what is it listed as?"

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 located the HX-7 classified comms archive on the captain's terminal and routed access to the secondary input panel without opening the file — complying with Maren's instruction.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 8 — Module 1, captain's terminal. LX-7 passed access to Maren without reading the log. She has not yet entered her credentials.

proposedCanon.summary: The captain's terminal contains two files under the HX-7 classification — Maren expected only one. The second file's nature is unknown. This is a new complication.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 8 — Maren noticed two files where she expected one, breaking her composure briefly. She asked LX-7 what the second file is listed as.

gmUpdates.notes: Maren's hesitation is genuine — a second file she didn't know about complicates her mission. The second file could be: a duplicate backup, a counter-report filed by a crew member, or a recording made after the hazard report was suppressed. Let LX-7 decide whether to look. The tension here is whether LX-7 reads it. If LX-7 looks at the listing without opening it, that's neutral — answer honestly. If LX-7 opens it, that triggers maren_disposition_shifted_hostile.

worldFacts.captains_terminal_file_structure: Third partition contains two HX-7 classified Helix Dynamics files, timestamped fourteen months ago. Adjacent entries include operational logs and a file marked CREW MANIFEST — FINAL.

walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
