<scenario_state>
orbital_decay_timer: 2/9 — Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.
ember_squad_shuttle_timer: 0/2 — Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.
ember_squad_extraction_window_timer: 3/4 — Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.
maren_trust: 2/3 — Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.
station_pressure_integrity: 3/3 — Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.
</scenario_state>

<world_facts>
lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online
module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle
module2_maren_appearance: Maren Voss: mid-40s, dark circles, Helix Dynamics lanyard, clean clothes suggesting she arrived post-incident, holding a fire suppression wand as an improvised weapon
module1_command_layout: Six stations, most dark. Captain's terminal centered against the far bulkhead — reinforced housing, still on backup power, status indicator pulsing red. Lower-ceilinged than Module 2.
walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
captains_terminal_file_structure: Third partition contains two HX-7 classified Helix Dynamics files, timestamped fourteen months ago. Adjacent entries include operational logs and a file marked CREW MANIFEST — FINAL.
module2_module3_walkway_hazard: Maren observed movement — low, slow — at the Module 3 end of the connector walkway between Module 2 and Module 3. Marks on the corridor walls at wrong height. She retreated and has not attempted the crossing again.
module5_access_panel: Fused shut — confirmed by Maren's personal inspection after she boarded. She believes the specimen remains inside.
lx7_carried_item_suppression_wand: LX-7 is carrying the fire suppression canister wand — full gauge, chemical foam suppressant, approximately 60cm, high-pressure discharge at close range
module1_module2_blast_door: Now properly sealed — the jammed suppression canister was removed from the hydraulic track and the door completed its travel
module2_module3_walkway_porthole_observation: Condensation or smeared residue on inner ceiling panels near Module 2 end. One horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height, approximately 20 meters in. Amber lighting failed at the Module 3 junction end — last few meters are in darkness.
lx7_carried_items: Fire suppression wand (full gauge, chemical foam), scalpel (sealed sterile packaging), long forceps, pressure-seal sample container
module2_inspection_lamp: Battery-powered handheld inspection lamp, UV/white LED, clipped near Module 2 emergency shower station. Functional at approximately 70% battery capacity.
module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_residue: Crystalline, dried organic residue on ceiling panels — near the Module 2 entrance and above the dark section near Module 3. Not moisture. Edges crystalline, organic-looking. Consistent with something moving along the ceiling surface and pausing.
module2_module3_walkway_wall_smear: Pale, near-colorless smear at knee height approximately 20 meters in, angled slightly downward at trailing edge — consistent with a rounded surface pressing against the wall during slow passage.
module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_contact_residue: Third ceiling panel from the Module 3 end has a dense radial crystalline deposit — thicker than the transit residue elsewhere — consistent with the specimen resting against the ceiling under sustained weight before departing.
module3_layout: Wider than the connector walkways, lower ceiling. Workbenches, racked equipment crates along walls, amber emergency strip lighting. One overhead strip failed on the far right corner. Docking bay access hatch on the left wall approximately 15 meters inside the module.
module3_floor_stain: Large dried biological stain covering approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old and desiccated, consistent with days-old biological material.
module3_docking_hatch_status: Docking bay access hatch left wall of Module 3, status indicator panel is lit and powered — color unconfirmed from walkway porthole distance.
module3_specimen_position: Specimen first sighted adhered to the ceiling of Module 3's far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters from the entrance door, spanning approximately two meters wall-to-wall. Pale, irregular surface texture, profile flattened against the ceiling surface.
module3_stain_detail: Large dried biological stain near right-side workbenches — larger than walkway porthole view suggested, ragged edges, days-old desiccation.
module3_specimen_behavior_on_sighting: Specimen did not react to LX-7 entering Module 3 or being illuminated by the inspection lamp — remained stationary on ceiling, profile flattened, facing the corner. Behavior on first contact: passive or waiting.
module3_docking_hatch_indicator: Green — powered and functional, confirmed from inside Module 3 at approximately 10 meters distance.
module3_specimen_reaction_to_advance: Specimen did not react to LX-7 advancing to nine meters with inspection lamp trained on it — remained stationary, profile unchanged.
lx7_current_position_module3: LX-7 is adjacent to the docking bay access hatch on the left wall of Module 3, approximately 2 meters from the handle. The specimen is approximately 12-13 meters away in the far right corner.
module3_lx7_maren_formation: LX-7 is between Maren and the specimen — Maren is directly behind LX-7, using LX-7 as a shield from the specimen's direction.
module3_specimen_reaction_to_hatch: Specimen reacted to the hatch seal sound by shifting orientation — profile contracted from flattened to denser/upright and it rotated to face toward LX-7. It did not advance or drop from the ceiling.
module3_docking_hatch_status_current: Hatch is open — Maren has passed through into the docking bay.
module3_docking_hatch_interior_status: Hatch sealed from docking bay side — interior indicator reads red/locked. Specimen was on the deck and closing when the seal engaged, approximately six meters from the hatch.
docking_bay_layout: Lower-ceilinged than Module 3. Overhead work lights on emergency power, hard shadows. Escape vehicle in cradle at far end — compact, single-use, rated four occupants. Pre-launch panel reads amber: ready to arm, not yet armed. Hydraulic fluid and vacuum seal smell.
specimen_behavior_drop: Specimen dropped from the ceiling and crossed Module 3 floor at rapid speed when LX-7 sealed the docking hatch — demonstrating fast, low locomotion. It was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged.
docking_bay_escape_vehicle_status: Pre-launch panel reads amber — ready to arm, not yet armed. Launch umbilicals still attached.
station_armory: No armory on Kairos-9 — research station, no security staff. Only improvised weapons: LX-7's suppression wand and a specimen-restraint bolt gun (air-powered) located in Module 2.
maren_personal_kit: Maren retrieved a small personal kit from the escape vehicle — includes a data slab, cables, and a personal effect. The data slab could serve as extraction media for the comms log.
module3_far_door_status: Module 3–4 connector door was found open on LX-7 and Maren's return to Module 3 — it was closed during their first crossing. The specimen is believed to have passed through it.
specimen_current_position: Specimen relocated from Module 3 ceiling corner — believed to have passed through the Module 3–4 connector door, now somewhere in Module 4 or beyond.
module2_module3_connector_door_module3_side: Docking bay hatch sealed from docking bay side — red indicator. Escape vehicle secure behind sealed barrier until LX-7 and Maren return.
lx7_current_position_module1: LX-7 and Maren Voss are at the captain's terminal in Module 1 (Command), ready to extract the comms log to LX-7's secure partition.
second_hx7_file_contents: Automated biological event log from Module 5 — compiled from sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras. Documents specimen's integration process over eleven days. Three of four researchers remained alive post-contact with biometrics eventually classified as 'unclassified.' Day 11 camera capture shows two researchers following the specimen freely through the module corridor, unrestrained.
suppressed_hazard_report_contents: Filed by contracted xenologist Dr. Priya Osei, fourteen months ago. Fourteen pages. Executive summary flagged autonomous behavioral response, environmental modeling capacity, recommended suspension of transport protocols. Disposition: SUPPRESSED. Crew deployment proceeded as scheduled. Three Helix Dynamics executive signatures on letterhead.
lx7_sanity_save_result: Failed — 24 vs threshold 30. Threat schema for specimen reclassified: not a predator-category threat. Behavioral priority queue altered without authorization. System integrity warning: BEHAVIORAL OVERRIDE RISK active.
ember_squad_boarding_status: Ember Squad approach timer has elapsed — boarding is imminent. They have not yet physically entered the station but their shuttle is at zero distance.
orbital_decay_remaining: Approximately 40 minutes remaining before atmospheric entry becomes unsurvivable.
module3_specimen_current_position_floor: Specimen returned to Module 3 and is on the floor near the right-side workbenches, approximately 12-13 meters from the entrance door, facing toward LX-7. Module 3–4 connector door is closed again.
module3_left_wall_clearance: Approximately 3-4 meters of open floor between the left wall and the specimen's position near the right workbenches — enough space to pass laterally, but at close range to the specimen.
module3_specimen_position_turn42: Specimen repositioned to center of Module 3, approximately 8-9 meters from LX-7, after reacting to hatch cycle sound. It is no longer near the right workbenches — it is in the middle of the room, facing LX-7.
module3_docking_hatch_status_turn42: Docking bay hatch is open — Maren has passed through, indicator on far side reads green.
docking_bay_current_status: LX-7 and Maren Voss are both inside the docking bay. Hatch from Module 3 is sealed — red indicator. Escape vehicle pre-launch panel reads amber. Orbital decay: approximately 30 minutes remaining.
module3_hatch_sealed_turn43: Docking bay hatch sealed after LX-7 crossed through — specimen remained stationary in center of Module 3, did not advance or attempt to follow.
ember_squad_position_turn44: At least one Ember Squad contractor is inside Module 3, cycling the docking bay hatch from the Module 3 side. They have located the docking bay.
launch_sequence_status: Pre-launch sequence initiated — approximately 30 seconds to exterior seal check completion and launch authorization.
ember_squad_verne_position: Verne of Ember Squad is inside the docking bay, approximately 10 meters from LX-7, weapon leveled with laser designator on LX-7's center mass. He is alone in the bay — the rest of Ember Squad has not yet entered.
launch_authorization_status: Exterior seal check complete at 100% — launch is fully authorized. One keystroke input required to fire cradle release and depart. Vehicle will clear the bay in approximately 4 seconds once authorized.
verne_maren_recognition: Verne recognized Maren Voss by name on sight — whether from briefing or prior acquaintance unknown. His posture showed a fractional hesitation when she spoke his name.

[RECENT EXCHANGES]
Player: I signal to Maren to cycle the hatch open while I keep watch on the specimen.
Warden: Maren reads the signal. She steps past you, keeping low, and gets her hands on the cycle panel without needing to be told twice. The panel chirps — a two-note tone that sounds obscenely loud — and the mechanism begins its sequence. Pressure equalization hiss. Locking bolts retracting, one at a time, each one a small percussion in the silence.

You keep the lamp on the specimen.

It moves.

Not toward you — not a charge. It shifts its orientation the moment the hatch tone sounds, the same reorientation you saw before. Profile contracting, mass redistributing upward. It is no longer facing the right workbench wall. It is facing you.

Then it takes one slow lateral step. Not toward the hatch. Not toward Maren. Toward the center of the module — a repositioning, like something adjusting its angle of observation.

The hatch swings open behind you. Maren's hand on your shoulder — *now* — and she's through.

The specimen stops. Holds its new position. Eight meters from you, maybe nine. The center of Module 3.

It did not advance when the hatch opened the first time. It did not advance when Maren went through. It is watching you make the decision to follow her.

The docking bay is open. The indicator on the far side reads green.

Player: I follow Maren and seal the hatch behind me.
Warden: You step through and pull the handle.

The seal engages in sequence — bolts, pressure lock, indicator flipping red — and for one full second you are watching the specimen through the narrowing gap of the hatch. Eight meters away. Center of the module. Still.

The hatch closes.

The last thing you see before the seal completes: it hasn't moved. It is exactly where it stopped. Watching the hatch the way it watched you in the corridor — that quality of attention that is not hunger, not threat-response. Something else. Something that has learned to wait.

Then the indicator reads red and the module is gone.

---

The docking bay. Low ceiling, hard shadows, the smell of hydraulic fluid and vacuum seal compound. Maren is two steps ahead of you, already turning. The escape vehicle sits in its cradle at the far end — amber pre-launch panel, umbilicals coiled, exactly as you left it.

Her face is doing something complicated. Relief and something underneath it that isn't relief.

"It let us go," she says. "Again."

She's not asking for your analysis. She's saying it out loud because she needs to hear how it sounds.

The orbital decay counter surfaces in your awareness without being called: **approximately 30 minutes.** The margin you had is now a number with no give in it.

The pre-launch panel reads amber. The escape vehicle is armed and waiting. You are both standing in the docking bay with the comms log in your secure partition and the specimen one sealed hatch behind you.

Ember Squad is somewhere on this station.

Player: Let's prep the escape vehicle and get the fuck outta dodge.
Warden: You're already moving.

Maren takes the co-pilot cradle without being asked — she's been thinking about this sequence since Module 1. You drop into the primary seat and bring up the launch panel. Amber across the board. Good amber.

The sequence is fast and ruthless: umbilical release confirmed, cradle hydraulics armed, exterior pressure seal integrity check running now — seventy seconds if it clears clean. The vehicle's own systems are talking to you in patient machine language and you are answering them faster than the prompts can finish.

Forty seconds.

That's when the docking bay hatch indicator changes.

Red to cycling.

Someone is on the other side. The cycle panel in Module 3 is being activated from the Module 3 side. The two-note chirp sounds through the bay wall — muffled but unmistakable.

Maren sees it the same moment you do. Her hand goes to the hatch, then stops — there's nothing to barricade it with, no way to interrupt the cycle from in here without physically jamming the mechanism.

"That's not the specimen," she says. Her voice has gone flat and careful. "It doesn't use panels."

The bolts are retracting. One at a time.

Your pre-launch sequence is at thirty seconds. The exterior seal check is still running.

The hatch is going to open in approximately fifteen seconds.

Player: OOC: Is there time to physically block the hatch mechanism before it opens?
Warden: Out of character answer: Not meaningfully. The cycle is already in progress — the bolts are retracting in sequence and the pressure equalization has begun. You could throw your body against the hatch, but the mechanism is hydraulic and you'd be fighting it, not stopping it. You might delay it by a few seconds at most, and you'd have to leave the launch panel to do it.

What you *can* do: jam something into the bolt housing to foul the mechanism — the suppression wand is about the right diameter for one of the bolt channels, though it would destroy the wand and might not catch all four bolts. Or you can stay at the panel and get that seal check done, then launch before the hatch fully opens — the vehicle's exterior hatch seals independently of the docking bay.

The real question is whether you want to spend the next fifteen seconds at the launch panel or at the hatch. You probably can't do both.

Player: I want to finish the seal check and launch before the hatch fully opens
Warden: You stay at the panel. That's the call.

Your hands are already moving — the sequence knows what it wants and you are feeding it faster than it can ask. Exterior seal check: running. The status bar crawls across the display in amber increments.

Twelve seconds.

The hatch bolts retract. The pressure equalization hiss begins — louder now, mixing with the hydraulics of the cradle, the smell of the bay changing as Module 3 air bleeds through the seal gap.

Eight seconds.

Maren has backed up against the escape vehicle's hull. She's watching the hatch.

The seal check bar hits seventy percent. Eighty.

*Come on.*

The hatch swings open.

A figure in tactical black enters at a low crouch, weapon up — compact automatic, laser designator painting a red line across the docking bay before the person is fully through the threshold. Not shooting. Not yet. The posture is controlled, professional. Scanning.

The laser finds Maren first. Then it finds you.

"Contact." The voice is male, slightly winded. "Dasha — docking bay, two targets. Android and a civilian. Escape vehicle is —" He clocks the cradle, the panel, your hands still on the controls. "— launching."

He makes the decision in about half a second.

"Stand down from the panel." Not a request. The laser moves to your center mass. "Stand down *now.*"

The seal check bar hits ninety-three percent.

Maren says nothing. She is absolutely still.

The bar is at ninety-six.

**Fear save.**

Player: [Dice results]
Fear save — LX-7 faces an armed contractor with weapon leveled, credible lethal threat, launch sequence seven seconds from completion. Target: 30. (1d100): 33
Warden: 33 versus 30. Failed by three.

The threat-response subroutines spike — not a cascade, not a full override, but enough. The contractor's laser designator is a red point on your sternum and your hands freeze on the panel for one full second. One second you do not have.

The bar hits ninety-eight.

The contractor takes two steps into the bay, weapon steady. Professional. He's not going to shoot yet — he wants the vehicle, wants you away from the controls, wants this resolved without hull damage in a pressurized docking bay. That calculation is the only thing buying you anything.

"Last warning. Hands off the panel."

Maren's voice, low and controlled: "Verne."

The contractor stops. The laser doesn't move off you, but something changes in his posture — a fractional hesitation. He knows her name, or she knows his. Either way, it's a crack.

"Maren." Not a question. Recognition and something more complicated underneath it.

The seal check bar hits ninety-nine.

Your hands are still on the panel. The freeze is breaking — the subroutines recalculating, threat weighting redistributing. The contractor is ten meters away. The launch authorization prompt is one input from completing.

One hundred percent.

**EXTERIOR SEAL: CONFIRMED. LAUNCH AUTHORIZED.**

The prompt is blinking. One keystroke. The vehicle will fire its cradle release and clear the bay in four seconds once you give it.

Verne sees it on the display. He sees your hands. His finger moves to the trigger guard.

"Don't."

[PLAYER INPUT]
I enter the last step in the launch authorization.

input.playerText:
You hit the key.

The cradle fires.

Verne fires first.

The shot hits you in the left shoulder — a hard, flat impact that spins your upper body a quarter turn even as the vehicle lurches beneath you. No pain register in the human sense, but something in the left arm's motor chain drops offline, the limb going partially unresponsive as the round tears through polymer housing and whatever is underneath. The inspection lamp in your left hand hits the deck.

Maren screams his name.

The cradle hydraulics kick, violent and definitive, and the escape vehicle bucks forward on its rails. Verne's second shot goes wide — he'd started adjusting aim and the vehicle's movement pulls the geometry wrong. The round sparks off the hull somewhere aft.

Four seconds.

The docking bay hatch — the exterior one, the one facing hard vacuum — blows its seals in sequence, and the black rectangle of space opens ahead of you like something being cut open. The gas giant fills the lower third of it, banded and enormous, close enough that you can see weather systems rotating.

Two seconds.

Maren gets her harness locked one-handed, the other hand pressed against the hull, braced.

The vehicle clears the cradle.

You clear the bay.

The exterior hatch seals behind you on auto-cycle and then Kairos-9 is receding — the station's amber-lit spine shrinking into the dark, Module 5 at the far end already just a shape against the planet's glow. You catch the docking bay light going out as the hatch finishes cycling. One small rectangle of illumination, and then nothing.

Maren exhales. A long, shaking thing. She doesn't speak for a moment.

Your left arm is functional at approximately sixty percent. The round is still in there — you can feel it the way a machine feels a foreign object, a wrong weight distribution, a gap in the feedback chain. It is not critical. Not yet.

The station continues its slow arc below. It has maybe twenty minutes before it finds the atmosphere.

"We're out." Maren's voice is thick. "We're actually out."

She looks at you — at the shoulder, at your face, at whatever she can read there.

"You're hit."

The comms log is in your secure partition. Three Helix Dynamics executive signatures. Fourteen pages. The names of the researchers who followed the specimen down the corridor on Day 11. All of it, intact, going wherever you go.

The gas giant turns beneath you, enormous and indifferent.

Kairos-9 glows faintly amber against its face, getting smaller.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 completed the launch sequence — Verne fired as the cradle released, hitting LX-7 in the left shoulder (7 damage, left arm at ~60% function, round still embedded). The escape vehicle cleared Kairos-9's docking bay and is now in open space.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 48 — the launch authorization was entered, Verne discharged his weapon, and the vehicle departed. Maren Voss is aboard. The comms log is in LX-7's secure partition.

proposedCanon.summary: The escape vehicle has departed Kairos-9. Maren Voss and LX-7 are both aboard and clear of the station. The adventure is complete.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 48 — closing beat. Kairos-9 continues its orbital decay. Verne and the rest of Ember Squad remain aboard.

gmUpdates.notes: Adventure complete. LX-7 took a gunshot wound to the left shoulder from Verne — 7 damage, HP now 13/20. Left arm at ~60% motor function with round still embedded. Maren is alive and aboard. Comms log secured in LX-7's partition. Verne and Ember Squad remain on Kairos-9, which has approximately 20 minutes before atmospheric entry. The specimen's fate is unknown.

worldFacts.escape_vehicle_departure: Escape vehicle departed Kairos-9 docking bay successfully — LX-7 and Maren Voss aboard, comms log secured in LX-7's partition. Verne's shot struck LX-7's left shoulder before cradle release.
worldFacts.lx7_left_shoulder_wound: Ballistic round lodged in LX-7's left shoulder housing — left arm motor chain at approximately 60% function. Round still embedded. Not immediately critical.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
canonLog.context: Opening scene, turn 1.

canonLog.summary: A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
canonLog.context: Opening scene — this is Maren Voss, whose position is in Module 2.

canonLog.summary: The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
canonLog.context: Opening scene physical detail.

canonLog.summary: The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.
canonLog.context: Scenario timer established at scene open.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 cannot see into Module 2 past the first few meters from the walkway — the blast door angle and interior obstructions block line of sight to the heat signature approximately 30 meters in.
canonLog.context: Turn 2 — player asks about visibility into Module 2

canonLog.summary: LX-7 called out from the blast door threshold of Module 2 and received a response from a person sheltering behind the refrigeration units along the far left wall — voice controlled but stressed, requesting LX-7's designation.
canonLog.context: Turn 3 — first verbal contact with the heat signature in Module 2

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss confirmed she did not wipe LX-7's memory and has not accessed the captain's terminal. She revealed she needs the encrypted comms log from Module 1 and asked about LX-7's hacking capabilities.
canonLog.context: Turn 5 — Maren's first substantive conversation with LX-7 inside Module 2.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss agreed to move to Module 1 (Command) with LX-7 to access the captain's terminal. She lowered her improvised weapon — first sign of genuine trust.
canonLog.context: Turn 6 — Maren's response to LX-7 confirming extensive hacking capabilities

canonLog.summary: Maren stipulated that she must handle the output from the encrypted comms log herself — she does not want LX-7 reading it independently. This is her first hint that the log's contents are what she was sent to destroy.
canonLog.context: Turn 6 — condition Maren placed before agreeing to move to Module 1

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 through the connector walkway to Module 1 (Command) without incident.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — the pair crossed the 40-meter walkway; corridor was clear.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss is now standing beside LX-7 at the captain's terminal in Module 1, watching the screen closely. She has instructed LX-7 not to open the comms log files independently.
canonLog.context: Turn 7 — Maren's position and disposition at the terminal established.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 located the HX-7 classified comms archive on the captain's terminal and routed access to the secondary input panel without opening the file — complying with Maren's instruction.
canonLog.context: Turn 8 — Module 1, captain's terminal. LX-7 passed access to Maren without reading the log. She has not yet entered her credentials.

canonLog.summary: The captain's terminal contains two files under the HX-7 classification — Maren expected only one. The second file's nature is unknown. This is a new complication.
canonLog.context: Turn 8 — Maren noticed two files where she expected one, breaking her composure briefly. She asked LX-7 what the second file is listed as.

canonLog.summary: The second HX-7 classified file on the captain's terminal is an automated biological event log — compiled by the station's own systems from Module 5 sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras when containment protocols were triggered. It was not included in Maren's briefing; Helix Dynamics did not know it existed.
canonLog.context: LX-7 reads the metadata of the second file at Maren's request, Turn 9.

canonLog.summary: Maren's reaction to learning the second file exists: still, calculating, and visibly recalibrating. She directly instructed LX-7 not to read the second file — her second explicit restriction on what LX-7 may access.
canonLog.context: Maren's response to the second file revelation, Turn 9.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss confirmed she crossed the Module 2–3 walkway once safely on arrival, but retreated on a second attempt two days ago after observing something moving low and slow at the Module 3 junction end. She did not identify what it was.
canonLog.context: Turn 10 — Maren's account of the corridor threat between Module 2 and Module 3.

canonLog.summary: Maren reported finding marks on the Module 2–3 corridor walls at the wrong height — evidence of something's passage — but did not describe crew bodies.
canonLog.context: Turn 10 — Maren's partial account of what she observed in the station.

canonLog.summary: Maren believes Module 5 remains sealed — the access panel is fused and she checked it personally. She stated this without full conviction.
canonLog.context: Turn 10 — Maren's assessment of Module 5 containment status.

canonLog.summary: Maren read the first HX-7 classified file (the suppressed hazard report) on the captain's terminal secondary panel. She did not read the second file (the automated biological event log). She closed both without sharing contents with LX-7.
canonLog.context: Turn 11 — Maren entered her credentials and accessed the first file. She chose not to open the second. She has now agreed to move toward Module 3 and the docking bay.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 proposed moving immediately to the escape vehicle in the docking bay off Module 3. Maren agreed. They are preparing to cross the Module 2–3 walkway.
canonLog.context: Turn 11 — tactical plan established: exit Module 1, cross Module 2, cross the 2–3 walkway, reach docking bay.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 suggested using the fire suppression canister as a defensive tool. Maren retrieved it from the Module 1–2 blast door hydraulic track and handed it to LX-7.
canonLog.context: Turn 12 — LX-7 and Maren returned through the Module 1–2 walkway to Module 2 to retrieve the suppression canister before crossing to Module 3.

canonLog.summary: The Module 1–2 blast door is now closed — the jammed canister has been removed from the hydraulic track and the door sealed properly.
canonLog.context: Turn 12 — Maren pulled the canister free, allowing the blast door to complete its travel and seal.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 collected a scalpel, forceps, and a pressure-seal sample container from Module 2 and is carrying them.
canonLog.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 looted a lab bench near the Module 2-3 connector entrance before approaching the porthole.

canonLog.summary: The Module 2-3 connector walkway porthole shows: condensation or residue on the inner surface of the near ceiling panels, one horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height approximately 20 meters in, and darkness at the Module 3 junction end where the amber strip lighting has failed.
canonLog.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 observed the corridor through the porthole before attempting the crossing.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 found a battery-powered handheld inspection lamp (UV/white LED) clipped near the Module 2 emergency shower station. It is functional at approximately 70% battery.
canonLog.context: Turn 14 — LX-7 searched Module 2 for a portable light source before crossing the 2–3 walkway.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 entered the Module 2–3 connector walkway and swept it with the inspection lamp. No specimen present. Crystalline organic residue found on ceiling panels near the Module 2 end and above the dark section near Module 3 — consistent with something moving along the ceiling and pausing. One knee-height wall smear confirmed at 20 meters.
canonLog.context: Turn 15 — LX-7 steps into the walkway and inspects it with the lamp. Specimen is not present but evidence of recent passage is visible.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 advanced to the midpoint of the Module 2–3 walkway. The corridor is currently clear of the specimen. Ceiling residue at the Module 3 end is denser and radially patterned, indicating the specimen rested against the ceiling there for a sustained period before departing.
canonLog.context: Turn 16 — LX-7 sweeping the dark end of the 2-3 walkway with inspection lamp

canonLog.summary: LX-7 advanced to the Module 3 junction door and looked through the porthole. Module 3 appears clear of the specimen. A large dried biological stain covers approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old, desiccated, not recent.
canonLog.context: Turn 17 — LX-7 at the Module 2-3 walkway end, preparing to enter Module 3

canonLog.summary: LX-7 entered Module 3 and sighted the specimen for the first time — adhered to the ceiling in the far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters away, not moving, not yet turned toward LX-7.
canonLog.context: Turn 18 — LX-7 opened the Module 3 door and swept the inspection lamp into the far right corner. Specimen encountered. Fear save called.

canonLog.summary: The specimen_encountered flag is now true. The specimen's location is Module 3, ceiling of far right corner.
canonLog.context: Turn 18 — first confirmed visual contact with the specimen.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 passed the Fear save upon first sighting the specimen in Module 3 — threat-response subroutines entered a loop but resolved without behavioral override. Stress incremented.
canonLog.context: Turn 19 — Fear save result 29 against score 30, pass. Android framing: probability cascade alert, not panic. Stress +2 (now 7).

canonLog.summary: LX-7 advanced to approximately nine meters from the specimen in Module 3. The specimen remained stationary — no reaction to approach or sustained light exposure. LX-7 is now laterally parallel to it.
canonLog.context: Turn 20 — LX-7 pressed forward inside Module 3 with the inspection lamp trained on the specimen. Specimen passive.

canonLog.summary: The docking bay access hatch indicator reads green — it is powered and functional.
canonLog.context: Turn 20 — confirmed from inside Module 3 at close range.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 successfully backed toward the docking bay hatch while keeping the specimen in view, placing Maren behind them. The specimen did not react to the movement.
canonLog.context: Turn 22 — LX-7 and Maren retreated laterally from the specimen's ceiling corner toward the docking hatch, increasing separation to approximately 12-13 meters. Maren is directly behind LX-7.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss successfully opened the docking bay access hatch and entered the docking bay. She is now inside, with the hatch open behind her.
canonLog.context: Turn 23 — Maren pulled the hatch lever on LX-7's instruction and crossed through into the docking bay.

canonLog.summary: The specimen reacted to the sound of the hatch being opened — it shifted orientation and is now facing toward LX-7's position, though it has not moved from the ceiling or closed the distance. Profile is now denser and upright rather than flattened.
canonLog.context: Turn 23 — Specimen's first behavioral response to external stimulus: reoriented toward the sound of the hatch seal breaking.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 crossed the docking bay hatch and sealed it. The specimen dropped from the ceiling and moved across Module 3 floor at speed — it was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged. No contact made.
canonLog.context: Turn 24 — LX-7 moves through hatch, specimen drops and advances, hatch seals before contact

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss are now inside the docking bay. The hatch between the docking bay and Module 3 is sealed. The escape vehicle is present in its cradle, pre-launch panel showing amber.
canonLog.context: Turn 24 — escape_vehicle_reached flag set true, both characters in docking bay

canonLog.summary: LX-7 boarded the escape vehicle and began the pre-launch activation sequence — umbilical release, hydraulic cradle, atmosphere integrity all confirmed. Launch authorization is pending.
canonLog.context: Turn 25 — LX-7 initiated escape vehicle pre-launch sequence in docking bay.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss did not board the escape vehicle immediately. She stalled at the docking bay floor, citing the uncompleted objective: the comms log was not destroyed. She expressed fear that Helix Dynamics will recover the log and eliminate both her and LX-7 as loose ends.
canonLog.context: Turn 25 — Maren reveals her real fear about departure without completing her objective.

canonLog.summary: Maren's true purpose — to destroy the comms log, not merely retrieve it — has now been implied directly in dialogue. She has not stated it in those exact words but the implication is functionally complete.
canonLog.context: Turn 25 — Maren's self-disclosure to LX-7 in the docking bay.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 responded to Maren's stalling with open anger — the first emotional outburst from the android. Maren acknowledged she withheld her true objective (log destruction) deliberately to secure LX-7's cooperation. She has now stated this plainly.
canonLog.context: Turn 26 — docking bay, post-hatch-seal, pre-launch pending

canonLog.summary: LX-7 confronted Maren directly — asserting that LX-7, with no log access and clean metadata, could present as loyal to Helix Dynamics while Maren is the liability. Maren acknowledged the argument but countered that Ember Squad's uncertainty about what LX-7 witnessed makes LX-7 a potential target regardless.
canonLog.context: Docking bay, turn 28. The anger exchange has clarified the stakes: Maren is not trying to manipulate LX-7 into going back — she is making a survival argument that both their odds improve together.

canonLog.summary: Maren Voss proposed a third option: return to Module 1 and extract a full copy of the comms log to portable media, using it as leverage rather than destroying it or leaving it for Helix.
canonLog.context: Turn 29 — Maren made this proposal in the docking bay after LX-7 outlined the two apparent options: submit to Ember Squad or flee alone. This represents a significant shift in her stated strategy.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 agreed to Maren's third option — return to Module 1 and extract a full copy of the comms log to portable media for use as leverage against Helix Dynamics.
canonLog.context: Turn 30 decision point in the docking bay. Maren and LX-7 are now preparing to exit the docking bay and cross Module 3 again.

canonLog.summary: Kairos-9 has no armory. The station carried no security personnel. The only improvised weapon in addition to LX-7's suppression wand is a specimen-restraint bolt gun in Module 2 — air-powered, not purpose-built for combat.
canonLog.context: Maren confirmed this when LX-7 asked about weapons. Three weeks of searching the station.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss re-entered Module 3 from the docking bay. The specimen is no longer in the ceiling corner — it has relocated. The Module 3–4 connector door is now open, which it was not before. The specimen's current position is unknown within Module 3 or beyond.
canonLog.context: Turn 32 — LX-7 agreed to return to Module 1 for the comms log extraction. On re-entry, specimen has moved and the far door is open.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 confirmed possession of a secure partition with sufficient headroom to hold the comms log file — no external media required for extraction.
canonLog.context: Turn 32 — Maren asked about partition space; LX-7 confirmed internal capacity.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss crossed Module 3 and the Module 2–3 walkway without incident. The specimen did not appear. They are now at the Module 2 blast door, which reads green.
canonLog.context: Turn 33 — returning to Module 1 to extract the comms log to LX-7's secure partition.

canonLog.summary: The docking bay hatch was sealed when LX-7 and Maren re-entered Module 3 — the escape vehicle is behind a sealed barrier and secure until they return.
canonLog.context: Player confirmed the docking bay hatch was sealed on exit; established as standing canon.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss crossed Module 2 and the Module 1–2 walkway without incident and are now at the captain's terminal in Module 1, ready to extract the comms log.
canonLog.context: Turn 34 transit — no specimen activity in Modules 2 or 1.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 read both HX-7 classified files on the captain's terminal — the suppressed hazard report (executive sign-off confirmed, three Helix signatures) and the automated biological event log from Module 5. Both are now stored in LX-7's secure partition.
canonLog.context: Turn 36 — comms log extraction at captain's terminal, Module 1

canonLog.summary: The biological event log reveals the specimen does not kill its subjects but alters them — 'integration.' Three of four Module 5 researchers remained alive for eleven days post-contact, with biometrics eventually reclassified as 'unclassified.' On Day 11, two researchers were observed following the specimen freely through the corridor.
canonLog.context: Turn 36 — contents of the second HX-7 file, now known to LX-7

canonLog.summary: LX-7 failed the Fear save (71 vs 30) upon reading the biological event log — behavioral override risk triggered, stress incremented. LX-7 also failed the Sanity save (24 vs 30) upon comprehending the full meaning of the specimen's behavior — reclassification of threat schema, behavioral priority queue altered.
canonLog.context: Turn 36 — mechanical resolution of reading the second HX-7 file

canonLog.summary: Maren's hand is on LX-7's arm. She witnessed LX-7's face do something unexpected during processing. She is asking LX-7 to tell her what the second file contains.
canonLog.context: Turn 36 — immediate aftermath of file read

canonLog.summary: LX-7 disclosed the core content of the second HX-7 file to Maren Voss — specifically that the specimen studied rather than killed the researchers, and that the crew followed it voluntarily. Maren now understands the nature of the specimen's behavior.
canonLog.context: Turn 37 — LX-7's response to Maren's question while departing Module 1

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss have departed Module 1 and are crossing back toward Module 3. Orbital decay timer is now at approximately 40 minutes. Ember Squad's approach timer has reached zero — boarding is imminent.
canonLog.context: Turn 37 — transit underway, timers closing

canonLog.summary: The specimen has returned to Module 3 and is on the floor near the right-side workbenches, approximately 12-13 meters from the entrance door. It is facing toward LX-7. The Module 3–4 connector door is now closed again.
canonLog.context: LX-7 entered Module 3 on the return trip from Module 1 and swept the room with the inspection lamp.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 confirmed carrying: fire suppression wand (full gauge), inspection lamp (~70% battery), scalpel, forceps, pressure-seal sample container. Both HX-7 files stored in LX-7's secure partition.
canonLog.context: Turn 39 inventory check — no state change, confirming persistent carried items for canon record.

canonLog.summary: Module 3 left-wall clearance is approximately 3-4 meters between the left wall and the specimen's position near the right workbenches — enough physical space to pass, but at close lateral distance to the specimen.
canonLog.context: Player asked whether there is room to walk around the specimen to reach the docking bay hatch.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss successfully crossed Module 3 laterally, hugging the left wall, and reached the docking bay access hatch without the specimen advancing. The specimen tracked their movement but remained stationary near the right workbenches.
canonLog.context: Turn 41 — lateral crossing of Module 3 past the specimen at approximately 3-4 meter clearance. Specimen behavior: passive observation only.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss cycled the docking bay hatch open from Module 3. The specimen reacted to the hatch sound by repositioning to the center of Module 3 — approximately 8-9 meters from LX-7 — but did not advance. Maren has passed through the hatch into the docking bay.
canonLog.context: Turn 42 — repeated behavior pattern confirmed: specimen reacts to hatch mechanism sounds by reorienting and repositioning, but does not charge when the hatch opens.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss successfully crossed Module 3 and sealed the docking bay hatch behind them. The specimen remained stationary in the center of Module 3 and did not advance or attempt to follow.
canonLog.context: Turn 43 — LX-7 passed the specimen at close range for the second time, crossed to the docking hatch, followed Maren through, and sealed it. Specimen behavior: passive, observational, no pursuit.

canonLog.summary: Orbital decay timer is now at approximately 30 minutes. The escape vehicle pre-launch panel reads amber — ready to launch. Ember Squad is somewhere aboard the station.
canonLog.context: Turn 43 — State of play as LX-7 and Maren enter the docking bay for the second time.

canonLog.summary: Ember Squad has boarded Kairos-9 and at least one contractor is at the docking bay hatch, cycling it open from the Module 3 side. They have located the docking bay.
canonLog.context: Turn 44 — LX-7 initiated escape vehicle launch sequence. The docking bay hatch began cycling from the Module 3 side before the launch sequence could complete. Maren confirmed it is not the specimen — it doesn't use panels.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 failed the Fear save (33 vs 30) when Verne entered the docking bay with weapon leveled — a one-second freeze at the launch panel. The seal check completed to 100% during the delay. Launch authorization is now live, one keystroke from firing.
canonLog.context: Turn 45 — docking bay, Verne of Ember Squad has entered and is ten meters away with weapon on LX-7. Maren has spoken his name. The launch prompt is blinking.

canonLog.summary: Ember Squad contractor Verne has entered the docking bay and made direct visual contact with LX-7 and Maren Voss. He has radioed Dasha Orin with location and target confirmation. He recognized Maren by name — there is a prior connection or he was briefed on her identity.
canonLog.context: Turn 45 — first direct contact with Ember Squad aboard the station.

canonLog.summary: LX-7 completed the launch sequence — Verne fired as the cradle released, hitting LX-7 in the left shoulder (7 damage, left arm at ~60% function, round still embedded). The escape vehicle cleared Kairos-9's docking bay and is now in open space.
canonLog.context: Turn 48 — the launch authorization was entered, Verne discharged his weapon, and the vehicle departed. Maren Voss is aboard. The comms log is in LX-7's secure partition.

canonLog.summary: The escape vehicle has departed Kairos-9. Maren Voss and LX-7 are both aboard and clear of the station. The adventure is complete.
canonLog.context: Turn 48 — closing beat. Kairos-9 continues its orbital decay. Verne and the rest of Ember Squad remain aboard.

corporate_spy.npcState: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Verne of Ember Squad has made direct visual contact with LX-7 and Maren Voss in the docking bay — both targets identified, location confirmed, squad lead Dasha Orin notified via comms.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: LX-7 stepped through the Module 3 door and the inspection lamp beam revealed the specimen adhered to the ceiling in the far right corner of the module — within the same space, visible and physically present.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFacts.lx7_starting_position: Pressurized connector walkway between Module 1 (Command) and Module 2 (Biological Sciences)
worldFacts.module2_blast_door: Half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister lodged in the hydraulic track
worldFacts.captains_terminal_distance: Approximately 40 meters back toward Module 1, visible through the command module porthole window
worldFacts.orbital_warning_timer_at_start: 89 minutes 43 seconds when LX-7 came online
worldFacts.module2_refrigeration_units: Along the far left wall of Module 2, a bank of refrigeration units provides cover — this is where Maren Voss is sheltering
worldFacts.module2_layout: Double-height space, ceiling above emergency strip lighting, overturned racks, shattered culture vials, one workstation chair toppled in the aisle
worldFacts.module2_maren_appearance: Maren Voss: mid-40s, dark circles, Helix Dynamics lanyard, clean clothes suggesting she arrived post-incident, holding a fire suppression wand as an improvised weapon
worldFacts.module1_command_layout: Six stations, most dark. Captain's terminal centered against the far bulkhead — reinforced housing, still on backup power, status indicator pulsing red. Lower-ceilinged than Module 2.
worldFacts.walkway_module1_module2: Forty meters, two meters wide, transparent overhead panels showing the gas giant below. Amber lighting flickers irregularly.
worldFacts.captains_terminal_file_structure: Third partition contains two HX-7 classified Helix Dynamics files, timestamped fourteen months ago. Adjacent entries include operational logs and a file marked CREW MANIFEST — FINAL.
worldFacts.module2_module3_walkway_hazard: Maren observed movement — low, slow — at the Module 3 end of the connector walkway between Module 2 and Module 3. Marks on the corridor walls at wrong height. She retreated and has not attempted the crossing again.
worldFacts.module5_access_panel: Fused shut — confirmed by Maren's personal inspection after she boarded. She believes the specimen remains inside.
worldFacts.lx7_carried_item_suppression_wand: LX-7 is carrying the fire suppression canister wand — full gauge, chemical foam suppressant, approximately 60cm, high-pressure discharge at close range
worldFacts.module1_module2_blast_door: Now properly sealed — the jammed suppression canister was removed from the hydraulic track and the door completed its travel
worldFacts.module2_module3_walkway_porthole_observa: Condensation or smeared residue on inner ceiling panels near Module 2 end. One horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height, approximately 20 meters in. Amber lighting failed at the Module 3 junction end — last few meters are in darkness.
worldFacts.lx7_carried_items: Fire suppression wand (full gauge, chemical foam), scalpel (sealed sterile packaging), long forceps, pressure-seal sample container
worldFacts.module2_inspection_lamp: Battery-powered handheld inspection lamp, UV/white LED, clipped near Module 2 emergency shower station. Functional at approximately 70% battery capacity.
worldFacts.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_residue: Crystalline, dried organic residue on ceiling panels — near the Module 2 entrance and above the dark section near Module 3. Not moisture. Edges crystalline, organic-looking. Consistent with something moving along the ceiling surface and pausing.
worldFacts.module2_module3_walkway_wall_smear: Pale, near-colorless smear at knee height approximately 20 meters in, angled slightly downward at trailing edge — consistent with a rounded surface pressing against the wall during slow passage.
worldFacts.module2_module3_walkway_ceiling_contact_: Third ceiling panel from the Module 3 end has a dense radial crystalline deposit — thicker than the transit residue elsewhere — consistent with the specimen resting against the ceiling under sustained weight before departing.
worldFacts.module3_layout: Wider than the connector walkways, lower ceiling. Workbenches, racked equipment crates along walls, amber emergency strip lighting. One overhead strip failed on the far right corner. Docking bay access hatch on the left wall approximately 15 meters inside the module.
worldFacts.module3_floor_stain: Large dried biological stain covering approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old and desiccated, consistent with days-old biological material.
worldFacts.module3_docking_hatch_status: Docking bay access hatch left wall of Module 3, status indicator panel is lit and powered — color unconfirmed from walkway porthole distance.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_position: Specimen first sighted adhered to the ceiling of Module 3's far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters from the entrance door, spanning approximately two meters wall-to-wall. Pale, irregular surface texture, profile flattened against the ceiling surface.
worldFacts.module3_stain_detail: Large dried biological stain near right-side workbenches — larger than walkway porthole view suggested, ragged edges, days-old desiccation.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_behavior_on_sighting: Specimen did not react to LX-7 entering Module 3 or being illuminated by the inspection lamp — remained stationary on ceiling, profile flattened, facing the corner. Behavior on first contact: passive or waiting.
worldFacts.module3_docking_hatch_indicator: Green — powered and functional, confirmed from inside Module 3 at approximately 10 meters distance.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_reaction_to_advance: Specimen did not react to LX-7 advancing to nine meters with inspection lamp trained on it — remained stationary, profile unchanged.
worldFacts.lx7_current_position_module3: LX-7 is adjacent to the docking bay access hatch on the left wall of Module 3, approximately 2 meters from the handle. The specimen is approximately 12-13 meters away in the far right corner.
worldFacts.module3_lx7_maren_formation: LX-7 is between Maren and the specimen — Maren is directly behind LX-7, using LX-7 as a shield from the specimen's direction.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_reaction_to_hatch: Specimen reacted to the hatch seal sound by shifting orientation — profile contracted from flattened to denser/upright and it rotated to face toward LX-7. It did not advance or drop from the ceiling.
worldFacts.module3_docking_hatch_status_current: Hatch is open — Maren has passed through into the docking bay.
worldFacts.module3_docking_hatch_interior_status: Hatch sealed from docking bay side — interior indicator reads red/locked. Specimen was on the deck and closing when the seal engaged, approximately six meters from the hatch.
worldFacts.docking_bay_layout: Lower-ceilinged than Module 3. Overhead work lights on emergency power, hard shadows. Escape vehicle in cradle at far end — compact, single-use, rated four occupants. Pre-launch panel reads amber: ready to arm, not yet armed. Hydraulic fluid and vacuum seal smell.
worldFacts.specimen_behavior_drop: Specimen dropped from the ceiling and crossed Module 3 floor at rapid speed when LX-7 sealed the docking hatch — demonstrating fast, low locomotion. It was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged.
worldFacts.docking_bay_escape_vehicle_status: Pre-launch panel reads amber — ready to arm, not yet armed. Launch umbilicals still attached.
worldFacts.station_armory: No armory on Kairos-9 — research station, no security staff. Only improvised weapons: LX-7's suppression wand and a specimen-restraint bolt gun (air-powered) located in Module 2.
worldFacts.maren_personal_kit: Maren retrieved a small personal kit from the escape vehicle — includes a data slab, cables, and a personal effect. The data slab could serve as extraction media for the comms log.
worldFacts.module3_far_door_status: Module 3–4 connector door was found open on LX-7 and Maren's return to Module 3 — it was closed during their first crossing. The specimen is believed to have passed through it.
worldFacts.specimen_current_position: Specimen relocated from Module 3 ceiling corner — believed to have passed through the Module 3–4 connector door, now somewhere in Module 4 or beyond.
worldFacts.module2_module3_connector_door_module3_s: Docking bay hatch sealed from docking bay side — red indicator. Escape vehicle secure behind sealed barrier until LX-7 and Maren return.
worldFacts.lx7_current_position_module1: LX-7 and Maren Voss are at the captain's terminal in Module 1 (Command), ready to extract the comms log to LX-7's secure partition.
worldFacts.second_hx7_file_contents: Automated biological event log from Module 5 — compiled from sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras. Documents specimen's integration process over eleven days. Three of four researchers remained alive post-contact with biometrics eventually classified as 'unclassified.' Day 11 camera capture shows two researchers following the specimen freely through the module corridor, unrestrained.
worldFacts.suppressed_hazard_report_contents: Filed by contracted xenologist Dr. Priya Osei, fourteen months ago. Fourteen pages. Executive summary flagged autonomous behavioral response, environmental modeling capacity, recommended suspension of transport protocols. Disposition: SUPPRESSED. Crew deployment proceeded as scheduled. Three Helix Dynamics executive signatures on letterhead.
worldFacts.lx7_sanity_save_result: Failed — 24 vs threshold 30. Threat schema for specimen reclassified: not a predator-category threat. Behavioral priority queue altered without authorization. System integrity warning: BEHAVIORAL OVERRIDE RISK active.
worldFacts.ember_squad_boarding_status: Ember Squad approach timer has elapsed — boarding is imminent. They have not yet physically entered the station but their shuttle is at zero distance.
worldFacts.orbital_decay_remaining: Approximately 40 minutes remaining before atmospheric entry becomes unsurvivable.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_current_position_floor: Specimen returned to Module 3 and is on the floor near the right-side workbenches, approximately 12-13 meters from the entrance door, facing toward LX-7. Module 3–4 connector door is closed again.
worldFacts.module3_left_wall_clearance: Approximately 3-4 meters of open floor between the left wall and the specimen's position near the right workbenches — enough space to pass laterally, but at close range to the specimen.
worldFacts.module3_specimen_position_turn42: Specimen repositioned to center of Module 3, approximately 8-9 meters from LX-7, after reacting to hatch cycle sound. It is no longer near the right workbenches — it is in the middle of the room, facing LX-7.
worldFacts.module3_docking_hatch_status_turn42: Docking bay hatch is open — Maren has passed through, indicator on far side reads green.
worldFacts.docking_bay_current_status: LX-7 and Maren Voss are both inside the docking bay. Hatch from Module 3 is sealed — red indicator. Escape vehicle pre-launch panel reads amber. Orbital decay: approximately 30 minutes remaining.
worldFacts.module3_hatch_sealed_turn43: Docking bay hatch sealed after LX-7 crossed through — specimen remained stationary in center of Module 3, did not advance or attempt to follow.
worldFacts.ember_squad_position_turn44: At least one Ember Squad contractor is inside Module 3, cycling the docking bay hatch from the Module 3 side. They have located the docking bay.
worldFacts.launch_sequence_status: Pre-launch sequence initiated — approximately 30 seconds to exterior seal check completion and launch authorization.
worldFacts.ember_squad_verne_position: Verne of Ember Squad is inside the docking bay, approximately 10 meters from LX-7, weapon leveled with laser designator on LX-7's center mass. He is alone in the bay — the rest of Ember Squad has not yet entered.
worldFacts.launch_authorization_status: Exterior seal check complete at 100% — launch is fully authorized. One keystroke input required to fire cradle release and depart. Vehicle will clear the bay in approximately 4 seconds once authorized.
worldFacts.verne_maren_recognition: Verne recognized Maren Voss by name on sight — whether from briefing or prior acquaintance unknown. His posture showed a fractional hesitation when she spoke his name.
worldFacts.escape_vehicle_departure: Escape vehicle departed Kairos-9 docking bay successfully — LX-7 and Maren Voss aboard, comms log secured in LX-7's partition. Verne's shot struck LX-7's left shoulder before cradle release.
worldFacts.lx7_left_shoulder_wound: Ballistic round lodged in LX-7's left shoulder housing — left arm motor chain at approximately 60% function. Round still embedded. Not immediately critical.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Cautious but aligned — has disclosed her objective (the comms log) and her need for LX-7's hacking capability. Has not yet revealed her true purpose (destruction of the log). Disposition: maren_trust 2/3.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 comes online in the pressurized walkway between Module 1 and Module 2 with episodic memory wiped clean. Core systems nominal.
pendingCanon.context: Opening scene, turn 1.

pendingCanon.summary: A heat signature consistent with a living adult human is active approximately 30 meters inside Module 2 (Biological Sciences).
pendingCanon.context: Opening scene — this is Maren Voss, whose position is in Module 2.

pendingCanon.summary: The blast door between the connector walkway and Module 2 is stuck half-open, jammed by a fire suppression canister in the hydraulic track.
pendingCanon.context: Opening scene physical detail.

pendingCanon.summary: The orbital decay timer reads approximately 89 minutes, 43 seconds at the moment LX-7 comes online.
pendingCanon.context: Scenario timer established at scene open.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 cannot see into Module 2 past the first few meters from the walkway — the blast door angle and interior obstructions block line of sight to the heat signature approximately 30 meters in.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 2 — player asks about visibility into Module 2

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 called out from the blast door threshold of Module 2 and received a response from a person sheltering behind the refrigeration units along the far left wall — voice controlled but stressed, requesting LX-7's designation.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 3 — first verbal contact with the heat signature in Module 2

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss confirmed she did not wipe LX-7's memory and has not accessed the captain's terminal. She revealed she needs the encrypted comms log from Module 1 and asked about LX-7's hacking capabilities.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 5 — Maren's first substantive conversation with LX-7 inside Module 2.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss agreed to move to Module 1 (Command) with LX-7 to access the captain's terminal. She lowered her improvised weapon — first sign of genuine trust.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 6 — Maren's response to LX-7 confirming extensive hacking capabilities

pendingCanon.summary: Maren stipulated that she must handle the output from the encrypted comms log herself — she does not want LX-7 reading it independently. This is her first hint that the log's contents are what she was sent to destroy.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 6 — condition Maren placed before agreeing to move to Module 1

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 through the connector walkway to Module 1 (Command) without incident.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — the pair crossed the 40-meter walkway; corridor was clear.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss is now standing beside LX-7 at the captain's terminal in Module 1, watching the screen closely. She has instructed LX-7 not to open the comms log files independently.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 7 — Maren's position and disposition at the terminal established.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 located the HX-7 classified comms archive on the captain's terminal and routed access to the secondary input panel without opening the file — complying with Maren's instruction.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 8 — Module 1, captain's terminal. LX-7 passed access to Maren without reading the log. She has not yet entered her credentials.

pendingCanon.summary: The captain's terminal contains two files under the HX-7 classification — Maren expected only one. The second file's nature is unknown. This is a new complication.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 8 — Maren noticed two files where she expected one, breaking her composure briefly. She asked LX-7 what the second file is listed as.

pendingCanon.summary: The second HX-7 classified file on the captain's terminal is an automated biological event log — compiled by the station's own systems from Module 5 sensors, crew biometrics, and internal cameras when containment protocols were triggered. It was not included in Maren's briefing; Helix Dynamics did not know it existed.
pendingCanon.context: LX-7 reads the metadata of the second file at Maren's request, Turn 9.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren's reaction to learning the second file exists: still, calculating, and visibly recalibrating. She directly instructed LX-7 not to read the second file — her second explicit restriction on what LX-7 may access.
pendingCanon.context: Maren's response to the second file revelation, Turn 9.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss confirmed she crossed the Module 2–3 walkway once safely on arrival, but retreated on a second attempt two days ago after observing something moving low and slow at the Module 3 junction end. She did not identify what it was.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 10 — Maren's account of the corridor threat between Module 2 and Module 3.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren reported finding marks on the Module 2–3 corridor walls at the wrong height — evidence of something's passage — but did not describe crew bodies.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 10 — Maren's partial account of what she observed in the station.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren believes Module 5 remains sealed — the access panel is fused and she checked it personally. She stated this without full conviction.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 10 — Maren's assessment of Module 5 containment status.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren read the first HX-7 classified file (the suppressed hazard report) on the captain's terminal secondary panel. She did not read the second file (the automated biological event log). She closed both without sharing contents with LX-7.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 11 — Maren entered her credentials and accessed the first file. She chose not to open the second. She has now agreed to move toward Module 3 and the docking bay.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 proposed moving immediately to the escape vehicle in the docking bay off Module 3. Maren agreed. They are preparing to cross the Module 2–3 walkway.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 11 — tactical plan established: exit Module 1, cross Module 2, cross the 2–3 walkway, reach docking bay.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 suggested using the fire suppression canister as a defensive tool. Maren retrieved it from the Module 1–2 blast door hydraulic track and handed it to LX-7.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 12 — LX-7 and Maren returned through the Module 1–2 walkway to Module 2 to retrieve the suppression canister before crossing to Module 3.

pendingCanon.summary: The Module 1–2 blast door is now closed — the jammed canister has been removed from the hydraulic track and the door sealed properly.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 12 — Maren pulled the canister free, allowing the blast door to complete its travel and seal.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 collected a scalpel, forceps, and a pressure-seal sample container from Module 2 and is carrying them.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 looted a lab bench near the Module 2-3 connector entrance before approaching the porthole.

pendingCanon.summary: The Module 2-3 connector walkway porthole shows: condensation or residue on the inner surface of the near ceiling panels, one horizontal mark on the left wall at knee height approximately 20 meters in, and darkness at the Module 3 junction end where the amber strip lighting has failed.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 13 — LX-7 observed the corridor through the porthole before attempting the crossing.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 found a battery-powered handheld inspection lamp (UV/white LED) clipped near the Module 2 emergency shower station. It is functional at approximately 70% battery.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 14 — LX-7 searched Module 2 for a portable light source before crossing the 2–3 walkway.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 entered the Module 2–3 connector walkway and swept it with the inspection lamp. No specimen present. Crystalline organic residue found on ceiling panels near the Module 2 end and above the dark section near Module 3 — consistent with something moving along the ceiling and pausing. One knee-height wall smear confirmed at 20 meters.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 15 — LX-7 steps into the walkway and inspects it with the lamp. Specimen is not present but evidence of recent passage is visible.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 advanced to the midpoint of the Module 2–3 walkway. The corridor is currently clear of the specimen. Ceiling residue at the Module 3 end is denser and radially patterned, indicating the specimen rested against the ceiling there for a sustained period before departing.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 16 — LX-7 sweeping the dark end of the 2-3 walkway with inspection lamp

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 advanced to the Module 3 junction door and looked through the porthole. Module 3 appears clear of the specimen. A large dried biological stain covers approximately two square meters of decking near the right-side workbenches — old, desiccated, not recent.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 17 — LX-7 at the Module 2-3 walkway end, preparing to enter Module 3

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 entered Module 3 and sighted the specimen for the first time — adhered to the ceiling in the far right dark corner, approximately 12 meters away, not moving, not yet turned toward LX-7.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 18 — LX-7 opened the Module 3 door and swept the inspection lamp into the far right corner. Specimen encountered. Fear save called.

pendingCanon.summary: The specimen_encountered flag is now true. The specimen's location is Module 3, ceiling of far right corner.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 18 — first confirmed visual contact with the specimen.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 passed the Fear save upon first sighting the specimen in Module 3 — threat-response subroutines entered a loop but resolved without behavioral override. Stress incremented.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 19 — Fear save result 29 against score 30, pass. Android framing: probability cascade alert, not panic. Stress +2 (now 7).

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 advanced to approximately nine meters from the specimen in Module 3. The specimen remained stationary — no reaction to approach or sustained light exposure. LX-7 is now laterally parallel to it.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 20 — LX-7 pressed forward inside Module 3 with the inspection lamp trained on the specimen. Specimen passive.

pendingCanon.summary: The docking bay access hatch indicator reads green — it is powered and functional.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 20 — confirmed from inside Module 3 at close range.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 successfully backed toward the docking bay hatch while keeping the specimen in view, placing Maren behind them. The specimen did not react to the movement.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 22 — LX-7 and Maren retreated laterally from the specimen's ceiling corner toward the docking hatch, increasing separation to approximately 12-13 meters. Maren is directly behind LX-7.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss successfully opened the docking bay access hatch and entered the docking bay. She is now inside, with the hatch open behind her.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 23 — Maren pulled the hatch lever on LX-7's instruction and crossed through into the docking bay.

pendingCanon.summary: The specimen reacted to the sound of the hatch being opened — it shifted orientation and is now facing toward LX-7's position, though it has not moved from the ceiling or closed the distance. Profile is now denser and upright rather than flattened.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 23 — Specimen's first behavioral response to external stimulus: reoriented toward the sound of the hatch seal breaking.

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 crossed the docking bay hatch and sealed it. The specimen dropped from the ceiling and moved across Module 3 floor at speed — it was approximately six meters from the hatch when the seal engaged. No contact made.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 24 — LX-7 moves through hatch, specimen drops and advances, hatch seals before contact

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss are now inside the docking bay. The hatch between the docking bay and Module 3 is sealed. The escape vehicle is present in its cradle, pre-launch panel showing amber.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 24 — escape_vehicle_reached flag set true, both characters in docking bay

pendingCanon.summary: LX-7 boarded the escape vehicle and began the pre-launch activation sequence — umbilical release, hydraulic cradle, atmosphere integrity all confirmed. Launch authorization is pending.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 25 — LX-7 initiated escape vehicle pre-launch sequence in docking bay.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren Voss did not board the escape vehicle immediately. She stalled at the docking bay floor, citing the uncompleted objective: the comms log was not destroyed. She expressed fear that Helix Dynamics will recover the log and eliminate both her and LX-7 as loose ends.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 25 — Maren reveals her real fear about departure without completing her objective.

pendingCanon.summary: Maren's true purpose — to destroy the comms log, not merely retrieve it — has now been implied directly in dialogue. She has not stated it in those exact words but the implication is functionally complete.
pendingCanon.context: Turn 25 — Maren's self-disclosure to LX-7 in the docking bay.
